description: The chemical dose rate expressed in milligrams per liter (mg/L) of water treated. This is the primary dosing metric used for regulatory compliance verification, CT calculations, and comparison against Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) limits.
reference: EPA SDWA; AWWA M20 Water Chlorination; 40 CFR Part 141

description: The exact date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the chemical dosing event commenced, as recorded by SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian. This is the principal real-world event timestamp for regulatory CT (Contact Time) compliance calculations.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian; EPA SDWA CT Compliance; AWWA M20

description: Indicates what initiated the dosing event: automatic SCADA/PLC control loop, manual operator action, scheduled dosing program, alarm response (e.g., low residual alarm), or regulatory requirement (e.g., CT compliance mandate).
reference: SCADA/PLC Operations; EPA SDWA; AWWA M20

description: The method by which the chemical was applied: 'continuous' (steady-state pump injection), 'batch' (fixed volume addition), 'slug' (rapid high-concentration dose), or 'intermittent' (cyclic on/off dosing). Affects dose efficacy calculations and regulatory reporting.
reference: AWWA M20 Water Chlorination; EPA Treatment Technique Guidance

description: Reference to the dosing pump asset used to inject the chemical, as registered in IBM Maximo CMMS. Enables pump performance tracking, maintenance scheduling, and dose accuracy auditing.
reference: IBM Maximo Asset Management (CMMS); AWWA M20

description: The total chemical cost in USD for this dosing event, calculated as chemical_mass_applied_kg multiplied by unit_cost_per_kg. Used for per-event cost allocation, treatment cost-per-MGD analytics, and OPEX reporting in SAP FI/CO.
reference: SAP ERP FI/CO; GAAP; GASB; AWWA Cost of Service Studies

description: The applicable Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) or Maximum Contaminant Level Goal (MCLG) for the target parameter as set by EPA or state primacy agency. Stored at event time for point-in-time regulatory compliance verification.
reference: EPA SDWA; 40 CFR Part 141; State Drinking Water Primacy Agency

description: Free-text field for the licensed operator to record observations, anomalies, equipment issues, or contextual information about the dosing event (e.g., 'pump cavitation observed', 'manual override due to analyzer failure'). Supports operational audit trail and incident investigation.
reference: Water Utilities O&M Procedures; EPA SDWA Operator Log Requirements

description: Actual measured residual concentration of the target parameter (e.g., free chlorine residual, pH) after the dosing event. Critical for CT compliance verification, MCL compliance, and dose efficacy reporting in Monthly Operating Reports (MOR).
reference: EPA SDWA 40 CFR Part 141; AWWA M20; MOR Regulatory Reporting

description: Measured concentration of the target parameter (e.g., chlorine residual, pH) immediately before the dosing event commenced. Provides baseline for dose efficacy assessment and regulatory compliance documentation.
reference: EPA SDWA; AWWA Water Quality Monitoring Standards; LIMS

description: The dosing pump flow rate in Gallons per Minute (GPM) during the event, as recorded by SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian via the pump tag. Used for pump calibration verification and dose accuracy auditing.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian; SCADA/PLC Operations; AWWA M20

description: The OSIsoft PI Historian or SCADA tag identifier for the dosing pump instrument (e.g., 'WTP1.PUMP.CL2.FLOW.001'). Enables direct linkage to real-time process data in PI Historian for trend analysis and audit trail verification.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian Tag Naming Convention; SCADA/PLC Operations

description: Indicates whether this dosing event is subject to regulatory reporting requirements (True = must be included in MOR, DMR, or CCR submissions). Enables automated filtering of reportable events for EPA and state primacy agency compliance reporting.
reference: EPA SDWA; NPDES DMR Reporting; CCR Requirements; State Primacy Agency

description: The planned or setpoint dose rate in mg/L as configured in the SCADA/PLC control system or operator dosing schedule. Compared against actual dose_rate_mg_per_l to assess dosing accuracy and control loop performance.
reference: SCADA/PLC Setpoint Management; AWWA M20; EPA SDWA

description: The desired or regulatory-required residual concentration of the target parameter after dosing. Compared against post_dose_residual_mg_per_l to determine compliance status. Derived from MCL/MCLG standards or internal treatment targets.
reference: EPA SDWA MCL/MCLG; 40 CFR Part 141; AWWA Standards

description: The stage in the water treatment process where the chemical was dosed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre_treatment|coagulation|flocculation|sedimentation|filtration|disinfection|post_treatment|distribution — promote to reference product]
reference: AWWA Water Treatment Plant Design; EPA SDWA Treatment Technique Requirements

description: Water turbidity in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU) at the dosing point during the event. Turbidity affects disinfection efficacy and is a key regulatory parameter under the Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR). Sourced from SCADA or LIMS.
reference: EPA SDWA SWTR; 40 CFR Part 141.73; AWWA Water Quality Standards

description: The unit cost of the chemical in USD per kilogram at the time of the dosing event, sourced from SAP MM purchase order pricing. Used for chemical cost allocation per dosing event and OPEX analytics. Classified as confidential due to supplier contract pricing sensitivity.
reference: SAP ERP MM/FI; GAAP; GASB Utility Accounting

description: The date and time when this chemical dose event record was last modified, including QA review updates, operator corrections, or regulatory annotation changes. Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.
reference: Water Utilities Data Governance Policy; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Total volume of chemical solution applied during the dosing event, expressed in liters. Used for chemical inventory consumption tracking, cost allocation, and procurement planning in SAP MM.
reference: SAP ERP MM Module; AWWA Chemical Consumption Tracking

description: The water flow rate in Million Gallons per Day (MGD) at the dosing point during the event, as recorded by SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian. Used to validate dose rate calculations and for CT compliance verification.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian; EPA SDWA CT Compliance; AWWA M20

description: The pH (Potential of Hydrogen) of the water at the dosing point during the event. Critical for CT compliance calculations (CT requirements vary with pH for chlorine disinfection), DBP formation potential, and corrosion control under LCRR.
reference: EPA SDWA; Lead and Copper Rule Revisions (LCRR); 40 CFR Part 141.72; AWWA

description: Water temperature in degrees Celsius at the dosing point during the event. Critical input for CT compliance calculations (CT requirements vary with temperature) and for chemical reaction rate modeling.
reference: EPA SDWA SWTR CT Tables; 40 CFR Part 141.72; AWWA M20

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each individual filter run record in the water treatment process. Primary key for the filter_run data product.
reference: AWWA Manual M37 – Operational Control of Coagulation and Filtration Processes

description: Reference to the backwash cycle record that was triggered at the end of this filter run. Links the filter run to its associated backwash event for end-to-end filter cycle analysis and backwash optimization.
reference: AWWA Manual M37; IBM Maximo Asset Management

description: Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Filter media (anthracite, sand, GAC) are consumable inventory items with material numbers, procurement lead times, and replacement schedules. Links filter performance tracking to material master for m

Foreign key linking to distribution.pressure_zone. Business justification: Filter runs produce water for specific pressure zones. Critical for water quality incident investigation (which zone received water from a filter with turbidity breakthrough) and hydraulic modeling. O
Reference to the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) or Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP) facility where this filter run occurred. Supports multi-facility reporting and regulatory compliance aggregation.
reference: EPA Safe Drinking Water Act (SDWA) 40 CFR Part 141; State Primacy Agency reporting

description: Foreign key linking to treatment.process_reading. Business justification: Filter run performance is monitored via SCADA/PI Historian process readings (turbidity, head loss, flow rate). filter_run captures many inline measurement values (influent_turbidity_ntu, terminal_head
Reference to the specific filter unit (granular media, membrane MF/UF, or GAC filter) on which this run was executed. Links to the asset management record in IBM Maximo.
reference: IBM Maximo Asset Management; AWWA Manual M37

description: Foreign key linking to asset.registry. Business justification: Filter runs occur on specific physical filter assets. Operators and regulators (Surface Water Treatment Rule) require linking filter run performance to the asset registry for condition trending, failu

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Filter turbidity requirements are mandated by specific regulations (LT2ESWTR, SWTR). filter_run has regulatory_compliance_flag and turbidity_threshold_ntu as plain text. Linking filter runs to the spe

The primary reason that terminated this filter run and initiated a backwash cycle. 'time' indicates the run reached its maximum configured duration; 'head_loss' indicates terminal head loss threshold was reached; 'turbidity_breakthrough' indicates effluent turbidity exceeded the threshold; 'operator_initiated' indicates manual operator decision; 'scheduled' indicates a pre-programmed backwash schedule; 'emergency' indicates an unplanned operational event.
reference: AWWA Manual M37; EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR)

description: The coagulant chemical dose applied upstream of the filter during this run, expressed in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Coagulation quality directly impacts filter performance and turbidity removal efficiency. Sourced from chemical dosing SCADA records.
reference: AWWA Manual M37; EPA Enhanced Coagulation Rule; OSIsoft PI Historian

description: The date and time when this filter run record was first created in the data platform (Silver Layer). Represents the audit trail creation timestamp, distinct from the operational run_start_timestamp. Used for data lineage and ETL audit purposes.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; GASB data governance standards

description: Identifies the originating system from which this filter run record was captured. 'scada_pi_historian' indicates automated capture from OSIsoft PI Historian; 'manual_entry' indicates operator-entered data; 'lims' indicates data sourced from the Laboratory Information Management System; 'hybrid' indicates a combination of automated and manual data.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian; LabWare LIMS; ISO 9001 Data Quality

description: Number of calendar days elapsed since the previous backwash cycle for this filter unit, at the time this run commenced. Used to assess backwash frequency compliance and filter maintenance scheduling.
reference: AWWA Manual M37; Plant Operations Manual

description: Duration in minutes of the filter-to-waste (ripening) phase at the start of this filter run. Null if filter_to_waste_flag is False. Used to optimize ripening time and minimize water loss while ensuring effluent quality compliance.

Indicates whether a filter-to-waste (ripening) phase was employed at the start of this filter run, diverting initial effluent to waste rather than the distribution system. Filter-to-waste is used to prevent turbidity spikes from entering the treated water supply during the filter ripening period post-backwash.
reference: AWWA Manual M37; EPA LT2ESWTR; AWWA Filter Performance Standards

description: Volume of water in Million Gallons (MG) diverted to waste during the filter-to-waste ripening phase. Contributes to Non-Revenue Water (NRW) calculations and water loss accounting. Null if filter_to_waste_flag is False.
reference: AWWA Manual M37; AWWA Water Loss Control Committee; EPA LT2ESWTR

description: Average flow rate through the filter unit during this run, expressed in Gallons Per Minute (GPM). Used to calculate hydraulic loading rate and assess filter performance relative to design capacity.
reference: AWWA Manual M37; OSIsoft PI Historian SCADA integration

description: The maximum allowable head loss in feet of water column configured for this filter unit, above which a backwash is automatically triggered. Stored per run to capture any threshold changes over time for audit and optimization purposes.
reference: AWWA Manual M37; Plant Operations Manual

description: The hydraulic loading rate applied to the filter during this run, expressed as Gallons Per Minute per square foot of filter surface area. A key operational parameter for filter performance assessment and comparison against AWWA design standards.
reference: AWWA Manual M37; AWWA Filter Design Standards

description: Turbidity of the water entering the filter at the start of the run, measured in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU). Provides context for filter loading conditions and is used in performance analysis and regulatory compliance reporting.
reference: EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR) 40 CFR Part 141.173; AWWA Manual M37

description: Turbidity of the filtered water (effluent) at the beginning of the filter run, measured in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU). Establishes the baseline effluent quality at run start for performance trending.
reference: EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR) 40 CFR Part 141.173; AWWA Manual M37

description: Differential pressure head loss across the filter media at the start of the filter run, measured in feet of water column. Establishes the baseline for head loss progression monitoring and backwash trigger evaluation.
reference: AWWA Manual M37; OSIsoft PI Historian SCADA integration

description: Free-text field for the certified water treatment operator to record observations, anomalies, or contextual information about this filter run. Supports operational knowledge capture and regulatory audit documentation.
reference: AWWA Manual M37; State Primacy Agency MOR requirements

description: Average particle count in the filter effluent during this run, expressed as particles per milliliter. Particle counting provides a more sensitive measure of filter performance than turbidity alone and is used for Cryptosporidium and Giardia removal credit under the LT2ESWTR.
reference: EPA LT2ESWTR; AWWA Manual M37; AWWA Particle Counting Guidelines

description: The highest turbidity reading recorded in the filter effluent during the run, measured in NTU. Critical for regulatory compliance — the EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule requires individual filter effluent turbidity not to exceed 1.0 NTU (or 0.3 NTU in 95% of measurements). Triggers turbidity breakthrough alerts.
reference: EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141.173; EPA LT2ESWTR; AWWA Manual M37

description: Average pH (Potential of Hydrogen) of the water entering the filter during this run. pH affects coagulation efficiency, disinfection effectiveness, and filter media integrity. Sourced from inline SCADA pH analyzers.
reference: EPA SWTR; AWWA Manual M37; OSIsoft PI Historian

description: Indicates whether this filter run met all applicable regulatory turbidity and performance requirements under the EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR) and LT2ESWTR. False indicates a compliance exceedance event requiring regulatory notification and corrective action documentation.
reference: EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; EPA LT2ESWTR; State Primacy Agency requirements

description: Total elapsed time of the filter run in hours, from run_start_timestamp to run_end_timestamp. Used for filter performance benchmarking, backwash frequency analysis, and O&M optimization. Populated upon run completion.
reference: AWWA Manual M37; AWWA Filter Performance Standards

description: The precise date and time when the filter run was terminated (by backwash trigger, time limit, or operator action), as recorded by the SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian system. Null if the run is still in progress.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian; AWWA Manual M37

description: Externally-known business identifier for the filter run, typically assigned by the SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian system or the WTP operations team. Used for cross-referencing with Monthly Operating Reports (MOR) and regulatory submissions.
reference: AWWA Manual M37; EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR) 40 CFR Part 141

description: Sequential count of filter runs for this filter unit since the last media replacement or major rehabilitation. Used to track cumulative filter usage, assess media aging, and plan media replacement schedules.
reference: AWWA Manual M37; IBM Maximo Asset Management

description: The precise date and time when the filter run commenced, as recorded by the SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian system. This is the principal business event timestamp for the filter run lifecycle. Used for run duration calculation and regulatory CT (Contact Time) compliance tracking.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian; EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR) 40 CFR Part 141

description: Current lifecycle status of the filter run. 'in_progress' indicates active filtration; 'completed' indicates normal termination; 'aborted' indicates premature termination due to operational issue; 'backwash_initiated' indicates the run ended by triggering a backwash cycle; 'filter_to_waste' indicates the run is in the filter-to-waste phase post-backwash.
reference: AWWA Manual M37; EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR)

description: The OSIsoft PI Historian SCADA tag prefix associated with this filter unit's instrumentation data stream. Enables direct linkage between this filter run record and the corresponding time-series data in the PI Historian for detailed process analytics and audit trail.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian; AWWA SCADA Security Guidelines

description: Turbidity of the filter effluent at the end of the run (at backwash trigger or run termination), measured in NTU. Used to assess whether turbidity breakthrough was the cause of run termination and for regulatory compliance documentation.
reference: EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141.173; AWWA Manual M37

description: Differential pressure head loss across the filter media at the end of the filter run (at backwash trigger or run termination), measured in feet of water column. Used to assess filter loading and determine if head loss was the backwash trigger. Compared against the head loss threshold to evaluate filter condition.
reference: AWWA Manual M37; OSIsoft PI Historian SCADA integration

description: Total Organic Carbon (TOC) concentration in the filter effluent during this run, measured in mg/L. Used to calculate TOC removal efficiency across the filter and assess GAC adsorption capacity. Sourced from LIMS or inline TOC analyzers.
reference: EPA Enhanced Surface Water Treatment Rule; EPA Stage 2 DBP Rule; AWWA Manual M37

description: Total Organic Carbon (TOC) concentration in the filter influent during this run, measured in mg/L. TOC is a key parameter for GAC filter performance assessment and Disinfection Byproduct (DBP) precursor removal tracking. Sourced from LIMS or inline TOC analyzers.
reference: EPA Enhanced Surface Water Treatment Rule; EPA Stage 2 DBP Rule; AWWA Manual M37

business_glossary_term: Turbidity Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) Exceedance Flag
description: Indicates whether the peak effluent turbidity during this filter run exceeded the regulatory Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) threshold of 1.0 NTU as defined by the EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule. True triggers regulatory notification and MOR documentation requirements.
reference: EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141.173; State Primacy Agency reporting requirements

description: The effluent turbidity level in NTU configured as the breakthrough trigger for this filter unit. When peak_effluent_turbidity_ntu exceeds this value, a backwash may be triggered. Stored per run to capture threshold configuration at time of run.
reference: AWWA Manual M37; Plant Operations Manual; EPA SWTR

description: The date and time when this filter run record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks data corrections, late-arriving SCADA data updates, or operator note additions. Used for data lineage and change audit trail.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; GASB data governance standards

description: Total volume of water processed through the filter unit during this run, expressed in Million Gallons (MG). A key performance indicator for filter throughput and backwash optimization. Sourced from SCADA flow meters integrated with OSIsoft PI Historian.
reference: AWWA Manual M37; EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR)

description: Average temperature of the water being filtered during this run, measured in degrees Celsius. Water temperature affects filter ripening time, head loss development, and biological activity in GAC filters. Sourced from SCADA instrumentation.
reference: AWWA Manual M37; OSIsoft PI Historian SCADA integration

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each finished water production record at the Water Treatment Plant (WTP). Primary key for this entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete daily/shift-level production event with a clear lifecycle.
reference: AWWA M36 Water Audits and Loss Control Programs

description: Foreign key linking to project.cip_project. Business justification: Production records during commissioning/startup track new facility performance against design capacity. Project closeout requires demonstration of design flow rate achievement. Links production data t

Foreign key linking to treatment.ct_compliance_record. Business justification: Daily finished water production records include CT compliance metrics (ct_achieved_mg_min_l, ct_required_mg_min_l) inline. These values are authoritatively owned by ct_compliance_record. Adding ct_com

Foreign key linking to distribution.pump_station. Business justification: Finished water is pumped into distribution via specific pump stations. Essential for tracking the physical handoff between treatment and distribution, hydraulic modeling, and water quality tracing. Op

Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Daily production records drive revenue recognition and are compared against budgeted production volumes. Required for monthly financial close, rate case revenue forecasting, budget variance analysis,

Foreign key linking to distribution.pressure_zone. Business justification: Finished water production is delivered into specific pressure zones. Critical for hydraulic balance, demand forecasting, NRW analysis, and MOR reporting. Distribution operators must track which zone r

Reference to the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) facility where this production record was generated. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE / MASTER_RESOURCE reference for TRANSACTION_HEADER role, linking production data to the specific treatment facility. Used for multi-plant production aggregation and facility-level reporting.
reference: AWWA M36; EPA SDWA Facility Registry

description: Foreign key linking to metering.installation. Business justification: Finished water production records must reference the master meter installation measuring total plant output for regulatory compliance. MOR/DMR submissions require certified metered production volumes.

Foreign key linking to distribution.storage_tank. Business justification: Daily production balancing and system hydraulics require tracking which distribution storage tank receives finished water output from each WTP production shift. Operators and planners use this link fo

Foreign key linking to asset.registry. Business justification: Finished water production records are associated with clearwells and storage assets (registered assets). Asset-level production tracking enables capital planning, condition-based capacity analysis, an

Timestamp when this production record was approved by the supervising operator for MOR reporting (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements and regulatory submission deadline tracking.
reference: EPA MOR Reporting Requirements; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Average finished water production flow rate during this production period, measured in Gallons per Minute (GPM). Derived from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian flow meter data. Used for hydraulic loading analysis, pump performance monitoring, and comparison against design flow rates.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian; AWWA Operational Best Practices

description: Volume of water used specifically for filter backwash operations during this production period, measured in Million Gallons (MG). A sub-component of plant operations water. Tracked separately for filter performance monitoring and optimization of backwash frequency and duration.
reference: AWWA Filter Backwash Recycling Rule; EPA 40 CFR Part 141 Subpart H

description: Indicates whether any unplanned or emergency chemical dosing event occurred during this production period (True = yes, False = no). Flags records requiring additional review in LIMS or SCADA systems. Supports operational exception reporting and chemical usage variance analysis.
reference: AWWA Chemical Handling Best Practices; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Average free chlorine residual concentration in finished water at the WTP effluent during this production period, measured in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Must meet the minimum residual disinfectant level of 0.2 mg/L at the point of entry to the distribution system. Critical for disinfection compliance and MOR reporting.
reference: EPA SWTR 40 CFR 141.72; SDWA Disinfection Requirements; EPA MOR Reporting

description: Minimum free chlorine residual concentration recorded in finished water during this production period, measured in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Tracked to identify potential disinfection deficiency events. Any reading below 0.2 mg/L triggers regulatory notification requirements.
reference: EPA SWTR 40 CFR 141.72; SDWA 40 CFR Part 141; EPA MOR Reporting

description: Timestamp when this production record was first created in the system (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Used for data lineage and audit trail.

Quality flag indicating the reliability of the production data in this record. 'GOOD' indicates validated sensor/meter data; 'SUSPECT' indicates data that may be erroneous pending investigation; 'ESTIMATED' indicates values estimated due to meter failure or outage; 'MISSING' indicates data gaps; 'CORRECTED' indicates values that have been manually corrected from original readings. Aligns with OSIsoft PI Historian data quality coding conventions.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian Data Quality Codes; ISO 9001 Quality Management; AWWA M36

description: Permitted design treatment capacity of the WTP for this production period, measured in Million Gallons per Day (MGD). Captured at the record level to support capacity utilization calculations even if the plant's permitted capacity changes over time. Used for regulatory capacity compliance reporting.
reference: EPA SDWA Operating Permit; State Drinking Water Program Capacity Requirements

description: Primary disinfection technology used during this production period. 'CHLORINATION' uses free chlorine; 'UV' uses Ultraviolet (UV) disinfection; 'CHLORAMINATION' uses combined chlorine (chloramines); 'OZONATION' uses ozone; 'COMBINED' uses multiple methods. Determines applicable CT requirements and DBP formation potential.
reference: EPA SWTR 40 CFR 141.72; EPA CT Guidance Manual; AWWA Disinfection Best Practices

description: Volume of water discharged to waste during filter ripening (filter-to-waste) operations following backwash, measured in Million Gallons (MG). A sub-component of plant operations water. Tracked for regulatory compliance with turbidity control requirements and water loss accounting.
reference: EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR); AWWA Filter Backwash Recycling Rule

description: Total volume of finished (treated) water produced by the WTP during this production period, measured in Million Gallons (MG). This is the primary production output metric used in MOR reporting, water balance calculations, and capacity utilization analysis. Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian clearwell/effluent flow meters.
reference: AWWA M36; EPA MOR Reporting Requirements; SDWA 40 CFR Part 141

description: Average fluoride concentration in finished water during this production period, measured in milligrams per liter (mg/L). The EPA Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) for fluoride is 4.0 mg/L; the secondary standard is 2.0 mg/L. The optimal fluoridation level recommended by HHS is 0.7 mg/L. Reported in MOR and Consumer Confidence Report (CCR).
reference: EPA SDWA MCL 40 CFR 141.62; HHS Fluoride Recommendation; EPA CCR Rule

description: Free-text field for operator or supervisor notes related to this production record. Used to document unusual operating conditions, equipment issues, source water quality events, regulatory exceedances, or other contextual information relevant to MOR reporting and operational review.
reference: AWWA Operational Best Practices; EPA MOR Reporting Guidelines

description: Maximum instantaneous finished water production flow rate recorded during this production period, measured in Gallons per Minute (GPM). Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian. Used for peak demand analysis, pump sizing validation, and hydraulic capacity planning.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian; Innovyze InfoWater Hydraulic Modeling; AWWA M31

description: Average pH (Potential of Hydrogen) of finished water at the WTP effluent during this production period. Optimal range is typically 7.0–8.5 for corrosion control compliance under the Lead and Copper Rule Revisions (LCRR). Critical for disinfection effectiveness (CT compliance) and distribution system corrosion control.
reference: EPA Lead and Copper Rule Revisions (LCRR) 40 CFR 141.82; AWWA Water Quality Standards

description: Ratio of finished water volume produced to source water volume intake, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9250 = 92.50% efficiency). Calculated as finished_water_volume_mg / source_water_volume_mg. A key operational Key Performance Indicator (KPI) for WTP performance benchmarking and water loss identification. Stored as a raw ratio to support analytics without recalculation.
reference: AWWA M36 Water Audits and Loss Control Programs; AWWA QualServe Benchmarking

description: Total volume of finished water consumed for internal plant operations during this production period, measured in Million Gallons (MG). Includes filter backwash water, filter-to-waste volumes, and other in-plant process uses. Used in water balance accounting and plant efficiency calculations.
reference: AWWA M36 Water Audits and Loss Control Programs; EPA MOR Reporting

description: Calendar date on which the finished water production occurred (yyyy-MM-dd). The principal real-world event date for this production record. Used as the primary time dimension for MOR reporting, water balance calculations, and daily production trend analysis. Serves as the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: EPA MOR Reporting Requirements; AWWA M36; SDWA 40 CFR Part 141

description: Externally-known business identifier for this production record, used in Monthly Operating Reports (MOR) and regulatory submissions. Format: WTP-PRD-YYYY-MM-DD-SHIFT. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: EPA MOR Reporting Requirements; AWWA M36

description: Current lifecycle state of the production record workflow. 'draft' indicates data entry in progress; 'submitted' indicates sent for supervisor review; 'approved' indicates validated and accepted for MOR reporting; 'rejected' indicates returned for correction; 'superseded' indicates replaced by a corrected record. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: EPA Monthly Operating Report (MOR) Guidelines; SDWA 40 CFR Part 141

description: Indicates whether any water quality parameter exceeded its Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) or treatment technique requirement during this production period (True = exceedance occurred, False = all parameters within limits). Triggers mandatory regulatory notification and public notice requirements under SDWA.
reference: SDWA 40 CFR Part 141; EPA Public Notification Rule 40 CFR Part 141 Subpart Q

description: Identifies the primary data source for the volumetric and quality measurements in this production record. 'PI_HISTORIAN' indicates automated ingestion from OSIsoft PI Historian; 'MANUAL_ENTRY' indicates operator-entered data; 'SCADA_DCS' indicates direct SCADA/DCS system feed; 'AMI' indicates Advanced Metering Infrastructure; 'HYBRID' indicates a combination of automated and manual sources. Supports data lineage and quality confidence scoring.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian; SCADA Best Practices; ISO 9001 Data Quality

description: Operational shift during which this production record applies. 'DAY' typically covers 06:00–14:00, 'EVENING' covers 14:00–22:00, 'NIGHT' covers 22:00–06:00, and 'FULL_DAY' represents a consolidated 24-hour daily record. Used for shift-level production analysis and operator accountability.
reference: AWWA Operational Best Practices; OSIsoft PI Historian Shift Reporting

description: Exact timestamp when the production shift ended (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used to precisely bound the production measurement window for SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian data integration and shift-level volume reconciliation.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian; AWWA Operational Best Practices

description: Exact timestamp when the production shift began (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used to precisely bound the production measurement window for SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian data integration and shift-level volume reconciliation.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian; AWWA Operational Best Practices

description: Total volume of raw source water drawn into the WTP during this production period, measured in Million Gallons (MG). Used as the input denominator for plant efficiency ratio calculations and water balance accounting. Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian intake flow meters.
reference: AWWA M36 Water Audits and Loss Control Programs; EPA MOR Reporting

description: Average temperature of finished water at the WTP effluent during this production period, measured in degrees Celsius (°C). Temperature affects disinfection Contact Time (CT) calculations, chlorine decay rates, and distribution system water quality. Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian.
reference: EPA CT Guidance Manual for Disinfection; OSIsoft PI Historian

description: Average Total Organic Carbon (TOC) concentration in finished water during this production period, measured in milligrams per liter (mg/L). TOC is a precursor to Disinfection Byproducts (DBPs) including Trihalomethanes (THMs) and Haloacetic Acids (HAA5). Monitored for Stage 2 Disinfectants and Disinfection Byproducts Rule (DBPR) compliance.
reference: EPA Stage 2 DBPR 40 CFR 141.620; AWWA Water Quality Standards

description: Primary treatment process technology employed at the WTP for this production record. 'CONVENTIONAL' includes coagulation/flocculation/sedimentation/filtration; 'DIRECT_FILTRATION' omits sedimentation; 'MEMBRANE' includes Reverse Osmosis (RO), Ultrafiltration (UF), or Microfiltration (MF); 'SOFTENING' includes lime softening; 'GROUNDWATER' includes well-based supply with disinfection only.
reference: EPA SDWA Treatment Technique Requirements; AWWA Treatment Technology Guidance

description: Average turbidity of finished water measured at the WTP effluent during this production period, in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU). Must comply with the Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR) maximum of 0.3 NTU in 95% of monthly samples. Primary finished water quality summary metric for MOR reporting.
reference: EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR) 40 CFR 141.173; AWWA Water Quality Standards

description: Maximum turbidity value recorded in finished water at the WTP effluent during this production period, in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU). The regulatory Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) for turbidity is 1 NTU. Tracked separately from average to identify peak exceedance events for regulatory reporting.
reference: EPA SWTR 40 CFR 141.173; SDWA MCL Requirements; EPA MOR Reporting

description: Timestamp when this production record was last modified (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Tracks data corrections and supervisor approvals.

Total volume of finished water pumped from the WTP clearwell into the distribution network during this production period, measured in Million Gallons (MG). Distinct from finished water produced — the difference represents storage changes. Critical for water balance and Non-Revenue Water (NRW) calculations.
reference: AWWA M36 Water Audits and Loss Control Programs; EPA MOR Reporting

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each process reading record in the silver-layer curated telemetry dataset. Primary key for the process_reading data product.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Design Standards

description: Reference to the treatment facility (Water Treatment Plant or Wastewater Treatment Plant) where this process reading was generated. Links to the facility master record for site-level aggregation and regulatory reporting.
reference: EPA NPDES Facility Identification; SDWA Treatment Plant Reporting

description: Foreign key linking to metering.installation. Business justification: Treatment process readings at flow measurement points reference the master meter installation measuring flow at that process stage. SCADA systems integrate process readings with meter installations fo

Reference to the physical instrument or sensor asset (e.g., flow meter, pressure transducer, turbidimeter, pH probe, chlorine analyzer) that generated this reading. Links to the asset management record in IBM Maximo for calibration tracking, maintenance history, and instrument lifecycle management.
reference: IBM Maximo Asset Management; AWWA M6 Water Meters; ISO 55001 Asset Management

description: Foreign key linking to distribution.pressure_zone. Business justification: Treatment process readings at clearwell and discharge points are monitored in context of destination pressure zone. Essential for CT compliance verification, pressure management, and SCADA alarming. O

Reference to the specific treatment process unit (e.g., clarifier, filter, UV reactor, chlorination chamber, RO train, GAC contactor) within the facility from which this reading originates. Enables process-unit-level performance analysis.
reference: AWWA Treatment Plant Design Standards; EPA LT2ESWTR Filtration Requirements

description: Reference to the physical instrument or sensor asset (e.g., flow meter, pressure transducer, turbidimeter, pH probe, chlorine analyzer) that generated this reading. Links to the asset management record in IBM Maximo for calibration tracking, maintenance history, and instrument lifecycle management.
reference: IBM Maximo Asset Management; AWWA M6 Water Meters; ISO 55001 Asset Management

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Process readings are taken to demonstrate compliance with specific regulatory requirements (MCLs, CT requirements, turbidity limits). process_reading has regulatory_limit_type and regulatory_limit_val

The SCADA alarm state associated with this reading at the time of capture. Reflects the process control alarm condition: normal (within operational bounds), low_alarm (below low setpoint), high_alarm (above high setpoint), low_low_alarm (critical low condition), high_high_alarm (critical high condition), sensor_fault (instrument malfunction detected). Used for operational response and incident tracking.
reference: AWWA SCADA Alarm Management Standards; ISA-18.2 Management of Alarm Systems; OSIsoft PI Historian Alarm Configuration

description: The data pipeline batch or job run identifier associated with the ingestion of this process reading into the Databricks Lakehouse silver layer. Used for ETL traceability, reprocessing identification, and data pipeline audit. Enables identification of all records loaded in a specific pipeline execution.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse ETL Audit Standards; OSIsoft PI Historian Connector Batch Processing

description: The date by which the instrument generating this reading is due for its next calibration verification. Sourced from the IBM Maximo asset management calibration schedule. Readings captured after the calibration_due_date may require additional quality review. Supports instrument reliability and regulatory compliance for monitoring equipment.
reference: ISO 9001 Measurement Equipment Calibration; AWWA Instrument Calibration Standards; EPA SDWA Monitoring Equipment Requirements

description: Indicates whether OSIsoft PI Historian exception and compression algorithms were applied to this data point before archiving. When True, the stored value may represent a compressed/exception-filtered point rather than a raw scan value. Important for understanding data density and potential gaps in high-frequency process data.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian Exception and Compression Documentation; AWWA SCADA Data Integrity Standards

description: The minimum CT (Contact Time for Disinfection) value in mg·min/L required by the EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR) for the applicable disinfectant, temperature, and pH conditions at this facility. Used to calculate CT compliance ratio (ct_value / ct_required) for regulatory reporting. Null for non-disinfection parameters.
reference: EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141 Subpart H; EPA CT Tables for Disinfection; State Primacy Agency Permit Conditions

description: The calculated CT (Contact Time for Disinfection) value in mg·min/L for disinfection process readings. CT is the product of disinfectant residual concentration (mg/L) and contact time (minutes), used to demonstrate compliance with the Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR) inactivation requirements for Giardia and viruses. Applicable only to disinfection process stage readings; null for non-disinfection parameters.
reference: EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141 Subpart H; EPA Guidance Manual for Compliance with Filtration and Disinfection Requirements; AWWA Disinfection Standards

description: Indicates whether this process reading is designated for inclusion in the Discharge Monitoring Report (DMR) submitted to the EPA or state agency under the facility's NPDES permit. True for effluent quality readings at WWTPs that must be reported on the DMR. Enables automated DMR data extraction.
reference: EPA NPDES 40 CFR Part 122; DMR Reporting Requirements; CWA Section 402

description: The unit of measurement associated with the measured_value field. Follows industry-standard engineering unit notation used in SCADA and PI Historian configurations. Common values include MGD (Million Gallons per Day), GPM (Gallons per Minute), PSI (Pounds per Square Inch), NTU (Nephelometric Turbidity Units), pH, mg/L (milligrams per Liter), and °C (degrees Celsius). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MGD|GPM|PSI|NTU|pH|mg/L|°C|%|mV|kW|gal|ft|ft/s|ppm|ppb|mS/cm|MPN/100mL — promote to reference product]
reference: AWWA Engineering Standards; OSIsoft PI Historian Unit of Measure Configuration; NIST SI Units

description: The maximum operational range limit for this process parameter as configured in the SCADA/PI Historian system. Values above this threshold may indicate sensor malfunction, process upset, or out-of-range conditions. Used in data quality validation and alarm management.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian Tag Configuration; AWWA SCADA Alarm Management Standards

description: The date and time when this process reading record was ingested into the Databricks Lakehouse silver layer from the source system (OSIsoft PI Historian or SCADA). Distinct from reading_timestamp (the real-world event time). Used for data pipeline latency monitoring and ETL audit trails.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Data Ingestion Standards; OSIsoft PI Historian Connector Audit

description: Indicates whether this process reading was manually entered by a licensed operator rather than automatically captured by the SCADA/PI Historian system. True for manual readings (e.g., grab sample results, field measurements, backup readings during SCADA outages). Required for data integrity auditing and regulatory compliance documentation under EPA SDWA.
reference: EPA SDWA Data Integrity Requirements; 40 CFR Part 141 Monitoring Recordkeeping; AWWA Operator Manual Entry Standards

description: Indicates whether the measured_value exceeds the applicable regulatory_limit_value for this process parameter. True when the reading constitutes a regulatory violation requiring notification, corrective action, and reporting under SDWA or NPDES permit conditions. Critical flag for compliance monitoring and MOR/DMR generation.
reference: EPA SDWA 40 CFR Part 141; NPDES 40 CFR Part 122; State Primacy Agency Reporting Requirements

description: The date of the most recent calibration verification performed on the instrument generating this reading. Sourced from IBM Maximo calibration records. Used to assess measurement reliability and support regulatory documentation of monitoring equipment maintenance.
reference: ISO 9001 Measurement Equipment Calibration; AWWA Instrument Calibration Standards; IBM Maximo Calibration Management

description: A human-readable description of the physical location within the treatment facility where this measurement point is installed (e.g., 'Filter Gallery - Filter 3 Effluent', 'Clearwell Inlet', 'UV Reactor Train 2 Outlet'). Supplements the tag_id with a plain-language location reference for operator use and regulatory documentation.
reference: AWWA Treatment Plant Operations Manual; EPA SDWA Monitoring Location Requirements

description: The minimum operational range limit for this process parameter as configured in the SCADA/PI Historian system. Values below this threshold may indicate sensor malfunction, process upset, or out-of-range conditions. Used in data quality validation and alarm management.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian Tag Configuration; AWWA SCADA Alarm Management Standards

description: The principal numeric measurement captured at the process control point. Represents the raw or lightly-conditioned sensor reading as stored in OSIsoft PI Historian. Units are defined by the engineering_unit field. Examples: flow rate in MGD or GPM, pressure in PSI, turbidity in NTU, pH value, chlorine residual in mg/L, temperature in degrees Celsius.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian Data Archive; AWWA M53; EPA SDWA MCL Monitoring

description: Indicates whether this process reading is designated for inclusion in the Monthly Operating Report (MOR) submitted to the state primacy agency. True for readings that represent regulatory compliance monitoring points required under the facility's operating permit. Enables automated MOR data extraction from the silver layer.
reference: EPA SDWA MOR Reporting Requirements; State Primacy Agency Operating Permit Conditions; 40 CFR Part 141

description: The category of process parameter being measured. Classifies the reading into a standard measurement type for cross-facility analytics and regulatory reporting. Examples: flow (volumetric flow rate), pressure (hydraulic pressure), turbidity (particle measurement in NTU), pH (acidity/alkalinity), chlorine_residual (disinfectant residual in mg/L), temperature (process temperature), chemical_feed_rate (dosing rate). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: flow|pressure|turbidity|pH|chlorine_residual|temperature|chemical_feed_rate|level|conductivity|dissolved_oxygen|TOC|UV_transmittance|BOD|COD|TSS|TDS — promote to reference product]
reference: EPA SDWA Monitoring Requirements; AWWA Water Quality Standards; 40 CFR Part 141

description: The name of the OSIsoft PI Historian server instance from which this reading was sourced. Water utilities may operate multiple PI servers across different facilities or regions. Used for data lineage, source system reconciliation, and multi-server federation queries.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian Server Configuration; Databricks Data Lineage Standards

description: The treatment process stage at which this reading was captured. Identifies the position within the treatment train for process control and regulatory compliance tracking. Key stages include intake (raw water source), coagulation, flocculation, sedimentation, filtration, disinfection (UV/chlorination/RO/UF/MF/GAC), finished_water (post-treatment), and distribution_entry (point of entry to distribution network). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: intake|coagulation|flocculation|sedimentation|filtration|disinfection|finished_water|distribution_entry|wastewater_influent|secondary_treatment|tertiary_treatment|wastewater_effluent — promote to reference product]
reference: EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR); AWWA Treatment Plant Design; 40 CFR Part 141 Subpart H

description: The OSIsoft PI Historian data quality status code associated with this reading. Indicates the reliability and validity of the measured value. 'Good' indicates a valid, reliable reading. 'Bad' indicates a sensor fault or communication failure. 'Uncertain' indicates the value may be unreliable. 'Substituted' indicates a manually entered or interpolated value. 'Shutdown' indicates the process unit was offline. 'ConfigError' indicates a tag configuration issue.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian Quality Codes; AWWA SCADA Data Quality Standards

description: The unprocessed, unconditioned sensor output value as received directly from the SCADA/PI Historian system before any silver-layer transformations, scaling, or quality adjustments. Preserved for audit and reconciliation against the source system. May differ from measured_value if scaling, offset correction, or substitution was applied.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian Raw Data Archive; Databricks Silver Layer Lineage Standards

description: The calendar date (yyyy-MM-dd) on which this process reading was recorded. Derived from reading_timestamp for partitioning, daily aggregation, and regulatory reporting purposes. Stored as a separate DATE column to support efficient date-based partitioning in the Databricks Lakehouse and daily compliance reporting.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Partitioning Standards; EPA Daily Monitoring Reporting Requirements

description: The silver-layer curation lifecycle status of this process reading record. 'raw' indicates the record has been ingested but not yet validated. 'validated' indicates the record has passed data quality checks. 'rejected' indicates the record failed validation rules. 'substituted' indicates the value was replaced by an operator-approved substitute. 'archived' indicates the record has been moved to long-term storage.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Quality Framework; AWWA Data Management Standards

description: The precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the process measurement was recorded by the SCADA/PI Historian system. This is the principal real-world event time for the observation, stored in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Distinct from ingestion or audit timestamps.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian Event Timestamp; AWWA SCADA Standards; EPA SDWA Monitoring Requirements

description: The configured sampling or scan interval in seconds at which this PI tag is polled or archived by the OSIsoft PI Historian system. Common intervals include 1 second (continuous process), 60 seconds (1-minute averages), 900 seconds (15-minute averages), and 3600 seconds (hourly averages). Used for time-series aggregation and data completeness assessment.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian Archive Configuration; AWWA SCADA Data Collection Standards

description: Reference to the SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) system instance that collected and transmitted this process reading. Identifies the specific PLC, RTU, or HMI node in the control network. Used for source system traceability and SCADA infrastructure management.
reference: AWWA SCADA Security Standards; ICS-CERT SCADA Security Guidelines; NIST SP 800-82

description: The operational system of record from which this process reading was ingested. Identifies the originating data source for lineage tracking and data quality assessment. Primary source is OSIsoft PI Historian (PI_Historian). Other sources include direct SCADA feeds (SCADA_Direct), manual operator entries (Manual_Entry), LIMS laboratory imports (LIMS), and AMI platform feeds (AMI).
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian Integration Standards; Databricks Data Lineage Framework

description: The original PI tag name or SCADA point name as it exists in the source system (OSIsoft PI Historian or SCADA). Preserved for data lineage and reconciliation purposes. May differ from the normalized tag_id if tag renaming or aliasing has occurred during silver-layer curation.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian Tag Configuration; SCADA Point Naming Standards

description: The reason code explaining why the measured_value was substituted or manually overridden from the raw_value. Applicable when reading_status is 'substituted' or quality_flag is 'Substituted'. Required for regulatory audit trails under EPA SDWA data integrity requirements. Null when no substitution was applied.
reference: EPA SDWA Data Integrity Requirements; 40 CFR Part 141 Monitoring Recordkeeping; AWWA Data Quality Standards

description: The specific treatment technology or process type associated with this reading. Identifies the treatment method for process-specific analytics and compliance reporting. Values include conventional (coagulation/flocculation/sedimentation), UV (Ultraviolet Disinfection), chlorination, RO (Reverse Osmosis), UF (Ultrafiltration), MF (Microfiltration), GAC (Granular Activated Carbon), ozonation, softening, and ion_exchange. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: conventional|UV|chlorination|RO|UF|MF|GAC|ozonation|softening|ion_exchange|biological|membrane_bioreactor — promote to reference product]
reference: EPA LT2ESWTR; AWWA Treatment Technology Standards; 40 CFR Part 141

description: The date and time when this process reading record was validated and promoted to 'validated' status in the silver layer. Null if the record has not yet been validated or was rejected. Supports data quality audit trails and SLA monitoring for data availability.
reference: Databricks Silver Layer Data Quality Framework; AWWA Data Management Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each source water intake event record in the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) operations system. Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to project.cip_project. Business justification: Intake structures are delivered via capital projects. Required for asset capitalization, linking intake permits to construction permits, tracking intake improvements, and connecting as-built drawings

Foreign key linking to project.construction_contract. Business justification: Source water intake structures are built or rehabilitated under construction contracts. Warranty claims, defect liability periods, and contractor performance tracking require linking each intake asset

Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Raw water acquisition and intake operations (pumping energy, screening, raw water purchase agreements) are discrete O&M budget line items. Budget officers track actual intake volumes and costs against

Foreign key linking to finance.grant. Business justification: EPA Drinking Water SRF and watershed protection grants are tied to specific intake locations and source water protection programs. Grant administrators and auditors require the intake-to-grant link to

Foreign key linking to metering.installation. Business justification: Source water intake events reference the meter installation at the intake point measuring raw water withdrawal volumes. Withdrawal permits require metered intake volumes for compliance tracking. Water
Identifier referencing the pump station asset through which raw water was drawn from the source. Links to the asset/facility master record managed in IBM Maximo CMMS.
reference: IBM Maximo Asset Management; AWWA M44 Distribution Network

description: Foreign key linking to treatment.process_unit. Business justification: Raw water intake events feed into specific treatment process units (e.g., raw water screening, pre-sedimentation, raw water storage). source_water_intake currently links to facility but not to the spe

Foreign key linking to quality.water_sample. Business justification: Source water intake events require raw water quality samples (turbidity, TOC, pH, algae) for source water assessment reporting and treatment optimization decisions. Role prefix 'raw_water_' distinguis

Foreign key linking to asset.registry. Business justification: Intake structures (screens, gates, pumps) are registered assets. Linking intake events to the asset registry enables maintenance scheduling, condition assessment correlation, and capital planning for

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Source water intake monitoring is mandated by specific regulations (source water assessment rules, withdrawal permit requirements). source_water_intake has permit_compliance_status as a denormalized p
Identifier referencing the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) that received the raw water intake. Links to the facility master record.
reference: EPA SDWA; AWWA M48; SAP ERP PM Module

description: Identifier referencing the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) that received the raw water intake. Links to the facility master record.
reference: EPA SDWA; AWWA M48; SAP ERP PM Module

description: Foreign key linking to distribution.storage_tank. Business justification: In some configurations, raw water intake pumps directly to distribution storage tanks (common in smaller systems or emergency interconnections). Operators track which tank received raw water for blend
Identifier referencing the regulatory water withdrawal permit under which this intake event was authorized. Links to the permit master record for compliance tracking.
reference: EPA SDWA; State Drinking Water Programs and Primacy Agencies; CWA Section 404

description: Identifier referencing the specific raw water source body or groundwater well from which water was withdrawn. Links to the water source master record (e.g., river, reservoir, aquifer, well).
reference: EPA Source Water Protection Program; SDWA Section 1453; Esri ArcGIS GIS

description: Algae cell count in raw water at intake, measured in cells per milliliter (cells/mL). Elevated algae counts indicate potential for taste and odor issues, cyanotoxin presence, and increased treatment chemical demand. Triggers source water protection alerts and treatment process modifications.
reference: EPA Cyanotoxin Guidance; AWWA M57 Algae; LIMS LabWare; State Primacy Agencies

business_glossary_term: Raw Water Conductivity (µS/cm) - Microsiemens per Centimeter
description: Electrical conductivity of raw water at intake, measured in microsiemens per centimeter (µS/cm). Serves as a proxy indicator for Total Dissolved Solids (TDS) concentration and overall ionic strength of the source water. Used to assess suitability for treatment processes such as Reverse Osmosis (RO).
reference: AWWA M48; EPA Secondary Drinking Water Standards; LIMS LabWare

description: Timestamp when this source water intake record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; GASB; EPA Data Quality Objectives; AWWA M48

description: Quality assurance flag indicating the reliability and validation status of the intake event data. 'validated' indicates data has passed QA/QC review; 'provisional' indicates data is pending final validation; 'suspect' indicates potential measurement anomaly requiring investigation; 'invalid' indicates data has been rejected; 'estimated' indicates values were estimated.
reference: EPA Data Quality Objectives (DQO); AWWA M48; ISO 9001 Quality Management; OSIsoft PI Historian Data Quality

description: Identifies the originating system or method by which the intake event data was captured. 'scada_pi_historian' indicates automated capture from OSIsoft PI Historian; 'manual_entry' indicates operator-entered data; 'lims' indicates data sourced from the Laboratory Information Management System; 'estimated' indicates values were estimated due to meter failure.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian; LIMS LabWare; AWWA M48; EPA Data Quality Objectives

description: Dissolved oxygen concentration in raw water at intake, measured in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Indicates biological health of the source water body and potential for anaerobic conditions that can release iron, manganese, and hydrogen sulfide, affecting treatment requirements.
reference: EPA CWA Water Quality Standards; AWWA M48; LIMS LabWare

description: Average flow rate of raw water intake during the event, expressed in Gallons Per Minute (GPM). Captured from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian flow meter readings at the intake pump station. Used for hydraulic modeling and pump performance analysis.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian; AWWA M48; Innovyze InfoWater Hydraulic Modeling

description: Geographic latitude coordinate of the raw water intake point in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for spatial analysis, GIS mapping of intake locations, and source water protection zone delineation in Esri ArcGIS.
reference: Esri ArcGIS GIS; AWWA M48; EPA Source Water Assessment Program

description: Geographic longitude coordinate of the raw water intake point in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for spatial analysis, GIS mapping of intake locations, and source water protection zone delineation in Esri ArcGIS.
reference: Esri ArcGIS GIS; AWWA M48; EPA Source Water Assessment Program

description: Date and time at which the raw water intake event concluded. Used with intake_timestamp to calculate duration and total volume withdrawn during the event.
Externally-known business identifier for the intake event, used in operational logs, regulatory submissions, and Monthly Operating Reports (MOR). Format: SWI-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.
reference: AWWA M48; EPA SDWA Section 1453 Source Water Protection

description: Method by which raw water was conveyed from the source to the treatment facility. 'gravity' indicates flow by gravity through an intake channel or conduit; 'pumped' indicates mechanical pumping; 'combined' indicates both methods; 'emergency_bypass' indicates use of an emergency intake route.
reference: AWWA M48; OSIsoft PI Historian SCADA

description: Operational condition of the intake screening equipment (bar screens, traveling screens) at the time of the intake event. Blocked or bypassed screens affect flow rates and may allow debris into the treatment process, impacting downstream equipment and water quality.
reference: AWWA M48; IBM Maximo Asset Management CMMS; OSHA 29 CFR 1910

description: Current lifecycle status of the intake event record. 'active' indicates intake is in progress; 'completed' indicates the event concluded normally; 'suspended' indicates intake was halted due to quality or operational issue; 'cancelled' indicates the event was voided; 'pending_review' indicates the record requires quality assurance review.
reference: AWWA M48; EPA SDWA Source Water Assessment

description: The principal real-world date and time at which the raw water intake event commenced, as recorded by the SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian system at the intake pump station. Conforms to ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian; AWWA M48; EPA SDWA

description: The regulatory Monthly Operating Report (MOR) reporting period (YYYY-MM) to which this intake event belongs. Used to aggregate intake volumes and quality data for submission to State Drinking Water Programs and Primacy Agencies.
reference: EPA SDWA; State Drinking Water Programs and Primacy Agencies; MOR Regulatory Submission Requirements

description: Free-text operational notes recorded by the plant operator regarding the intake event, including any anomalies, equipment issues, unusual source water conditions, or actions taken. Supports operational log requirements and regulatory audit trails.
reference: AWWA M48; EPA SDWA Operator Log Requirements; State Primacy Agencies

description: Count of intake pumps operating simultaneously during the intake event. Used for energy consumption analysis, pump performance benchmarking, and maintenance scheduling in IBM Maximo CMMS.
reference: IBM Maximo Asset Management CMMS; AWWA M44; OSIsoft PI Historian

business_glossary_term: Permit Daily Withdrawal Limit (MG) - Million Gallons
description: Maximum daily volume of raw water authorized for withdrawal under the applicable water withdrawal permit, expressed in Million Gallons (MG). Used to assess compliance status against actual volume withdrawn.
reference: State Drinking Water Programs and Primacy Agencies; EPA SDWA; CWA

business_glossary_term: Raw Water pH (Potential of Hydrogen) Level
description: pH (Potential of Hydrogen) of raw water at the point of intake, measured on the standard 0-14 scale. Influences coagulation efficiency, disinfection effectiveness, and corrosion control treatment. Monitored per Lead and Copper Rule Revisions (LCRR) requirements.
reference: EPA Lead and Copper Rule Revisions (LCRR); AWWA M48; LIMS LabWare; OSIsoft PI Historian

business_glossary_term: Pump Station Operating Pressure (PSI) - Pounds per Square Inch
description: Operating pressure at the intake pump station during the intake event, measured in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI). Monitored via SCADA to ensure pumps operate within design parameters and to detect potential main breaks or pressure anomalies.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian SCADA; AWWA M44; OSHA 29 CFR 1910

description: Apparent color of raw water at intake, measured in Color Units (CPU) using the platinum-cobalt standard. High color values indicate elevated natural organic matter (NOM) and humic substances, which are precursors to Disinfection Byproducts (DBP) including Trihalomethanes (THM) and Haloacetic Acids (HAA5).
reference: EPA Stage 2 DBPR; AWWA M48; LIMS LabWare; Standard Methods for Examination of Water and Wastewater

description: Classification of the raw water source type. Determines applicable treatment processes, regulatory monitoring requirements, and source water assessment protocols under SDWA. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: surface_water|groundwater|purchased_water|recycled_water|brackish_water|other — promote to reference product]
reference: EPA SDWA Section 1453; AWWA M48; State Primacy Agencies

description: Indicates whether an active source water quality alert or advisory was in effect at the time of this intake event (e.g., algal bloom advisory, upstream contamination notice, drought restriction). When True, triggers enhanced monitoring protocols and treatment process review.
reference: EPA Source Water Protection Program; AWWA M48; State Primacy Agencies

description: Designation of the source water protection zone or wellhead protection area associated with this intake point, as defined in the utility's Source Water Assessment and Protection (SWAP) program. Used for land use management and contamination risk assessment.
reference: EPA SDWA Section 1453 Source Water Protection; State Primacy Agencies; Esri ArcGIS GIS

description: Total Dissolved Solids (TDS) concentration of raw water at intake, measured in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Indicates overall mineral content and salinity of the source water. Drives decisions on treatment technology selection including Reverse Osmosis (RO) and ion exchange.
reference: EPA Secondary Drinking Water Standards (SMCL); AWWA M48; LIMS LabWare

description: Temperature of raw water at the point of intake, measured in degrees Celsius. Affects disinfection Contact Time (CT) calculations, chemical dosing rates, biological activity, and seasonal treatment process adjustments.
reference: EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR); AWWA M48; OSIsoft PI Historian SCADA

description: Total Organic Carbon (TOC) concentration of raw water at intake, measured in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Critical parameter for predicting Disinfection Byproduct (DBP) formation potential including Trihalomethanes (THM) and Haloacetic Acids (HAA5). Drives treatment process adjustments.
reference: EPA Stage 2 Disinfectants and Disinfection Byproducts Rule (Stage 2 DBPR); EPA Enhanced Coagulation Rule; AWWA M48; LIMS LabWare

business_glossary_term: Raw Water Turbidity (NTU) - Nephelometric Turbidity Units
description: Turbidity of raw water at the point of intake, measured in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU). A key raw water quality parameter that drives coagulation chemical dosing decisions and treatment process optimization. Captured at intake via inline turbidimeter or LIMS sample.
reference: EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR); AWWA M37; LIMS LabWare; OSIsoft PI Historian

description: Timestamp when this source water intake record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Tracks data corrections, QA/QC updates, and regulatory review amendments. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; GASB; EPA Data Quality Objectives; AWWA M48

description: Free-text description of any known upstream events or conditions that may affect raw water quality at the time of intake (e.g., upstream spill, agricultural runoff, upstream WWTP discharge, construction activity). Supports source water protection program documentation.
reference: EPA Source Water Assessment Program (SWAP); AWWA M48; SDWA Section 1453

description: Total volume of raw water withdrawn during the intake event, expressed in Million Gallons (MG). This is the principal quantitative measurement for the intake event, used for permit compliance, production planning, and Non-Revenue Water (NRW) calculations.
reference: AWWA M36 Water Audits; EPA SDWA; State Withdrawal Permit Conditions

description: Prevailing weather condition at the time of the intake event. Weather conditions significantly influence raw water quality parameters (turbidity spikes during storm events, algae blooms during drought/heat). Used for source water quality trend analysis and treatment process correlation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|rain|heavy_rain|storm|snow|drought|flood_event — promote to reference product]
reference: AWWA M48 Source Water Protection; EPA Source Water Assessment Program (SWAP)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each CT compliance calculation record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete regulatory compliance calculation event with a clear lifecycle under the Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR).
reference: AWWA; U.S. EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR) 40 CFR Part 141

description: Foreign key linking to treatment.chemical_dose_event. Business justification: CT (Contact Time) compliance is calculated based on the disinfectant concentration achieved by a specific chemical dosing event. Linking ct_compliance_record to the triggering chemical_dose_event prov

Foreign key linking to distribution.pressure_zone. Business justification: CT compliance is calculated for water entering specific pressure zones. Regulatory requirement: MOR submissions must demonstrate adequate disinfection for each zone served. Different zones have differ

Reference to the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) or Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP) facility where this CT compliance calculation was performed. Links to the facility master record in the asset management domain.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; ISO 55001 Asset Management

description: Reference to the specific treatment unit or disinfection contact basin within the WTP where CT was measured (e.g., chlorine contact chamber, UV reactor, ozone contactor). Sourced from OSIsoft PI Historian asset hierarchy or IBM Maximo asset register.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; AWWA; OSIsoft PI Historian

description: Foreign key linking to treatment.process_reading. Business justification: CT compliance calculations use real-time SCADA/PI Historian readings for disinfectant concentration and flow rate. ct_compliance_record has pi_tag_concentration and pi_tag_flow as string tag reference

Foreign key linking to asset.registry. Business justification: CT compliance is calculated for specific disinfection contact chambers (registered assets). SWTR regulatory audit trails require linking CT records to the physical contact chamber asset for asset-leve

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: CT compliance records demonstrate compliance with the Surface Water Treatment Rule — a specific regulatory requirement. ct_compliance_record has ct_table_reference and target_organism as plain text. A

Foreign key linking to treatment.source_water_intake. Business justification: CT compliance requirements under the Surface Water Treatment Rule depend on source water type (surface vs. groundwater), temperature, and turbidity — all captured in source_water_intake. ct_compliance

Foreign key linking to quality.water_sample. Business justification: CT compliance calculations (SWTR disinfection) are supported by water samples measuring pH, temperature, and disinfectant concentration. Linking ct_compliance_record to the supporting water_sample pro

The dimensionless baffling factor (T10/T) applied to the theoretical hydraulic detention time to calculate the effective T10 contact time. Ranges from 0.1 (poor baffling) to 1.0 (plug flow). Determined by tracer study or EPA baffling factor table per SWTR guidance.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR Guidance Manual; AWWA

description: The method used to derive the CT compliance calculation. 'scada_automated' = real-time automated calculation from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian; 'manual_grab' = operator-collected grab sample data; 'lims_integrated' = data sourced from LIMS (LabWare); 'pi_historian_batch' = batch calculation from PI Historian archived data.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; OSIsoft PI Historian; LIMS (LabWare)

description: The principal real-world timestamp when the CT compliance calculation was performed or the SCADA/PI Historian data snapshot was captured. Represents the business event time, distinct from record audit timestamps. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; OSIsoft PI Historian

description: Free-text field for operator or supervisor notes regarding this CT compliance calculation, such as unusual operating conditions, equipment anomalies, data quality flags, or explanations for deviations. Supports MOR narrative documentation.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; AWWA; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Current lifecycle status of this CT compliance calculation record indicating whether the calculated CT ratio meets regulatory requirements. 'pass' = CT ratio >= 1.0; 'fail' = CT ratio < 1.0 triggering corrective action; 'pending_review' = awaiting operator verification; 'exceedance_reported' = violation reported to primacy agency; 'data_gap' = missing data prevents calculation.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; State Drinking Water Programs and Primacy Agencies

description: The effective hydraulic contact time (T10 — time for 10% of tracer to pass through) in minutes for the disinfection contact basin or reactor at the measured flow rate. T10 is the regulatory-accepted contact time per EPA guidance, accounting for short-circuiting.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; AWWA Disinfection Committee

description: Free-text description of the corrective action taken or planned in response to a CT compliance failure, such as increasing disinfectant dosage, reducing flow rate, or repairing baffling structures. Populated when corrective_action_required = True.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; AWWA; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Indicates whether a corrective action plan is required as a result of this CT compliance record (True = corrective action required due to compliance failure; False = no corrective action required). Triggers workflow in IBM Maximo or SAP PM for corrective maintenance or process adjustment.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; IBM Maximo Asset Management; SAP ERP PM

description: Audit timestamp indicating when this CT compliance record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

The actual CT (Contact Time for Disinfection) value achieved during the measurement period, expressed in mg/L·min (disinfectant concentration multiplied by contact time). This is the primary quantitative result of the compliance calculation. Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian real-time process data.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; AWWA; OSIsoft PI Historian

description: The ratio of CT calculated to CT required (ct_calculated / ct_required). A value >= 1.0 indicates compliance (pass); a value < 1.0 indicates a compliance failure. Critical metric for MOR regulatory submissions and EPA primacy agency reporting under SWTR.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; State Drinking Water Programs and Primacy Agencies

description: The minimum CT value required by regulation to achieve the target log inactivation credit for Giardia lamblia and/or viruses, expressed in mg/L·min. Derived from EPA CT tables based on disinfectant type, temperature, and pH. Used as the denominator in the CT ratio calculation.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141 Appendix C; AWWA

description: The specific EPA CT table identifier used to determine the required CT value for this calculation (e.g., 'Table C-1 Free Chlorine Giardia', 'Table C-3 Chloramines Virus'). Provides regulatory traceability for the ct_required value and supports MOR documentation.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141 Appendix C; AWWA

description: The operational system of record from which the raw measurement data for this CT calculation was sourced. Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements. 'pi_historian' = OSIsoft PI Historian; 'lims' = LabWare LIMS; 'scada_manual' = SCADA HMI manual entry; 'hmi_entry' = Human Machine Interface direct entry; 'imported' = external data import.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian; LIMS (LabWare); ISO 9001 Quality Management
