description: The measured residual concentration of the disinfectant at the point of CT measurement, expressed in mg/L. For free chlorine, this is the free chlorine residual; for chloramines, the total chloramine residual. Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian or LIMS grab sample results.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; AWWA; OSIsoft PI Historian

description: The type of primary disinfectant used in the treatment process for this CT calculation. Determines which EPA CT table is applied to derive the required CT value. UV (Ultraviolet Disinfection) is expressed as UV dose (mJ/cm²) rather than concentration-based CT.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; AWWA

business_glossary_term: Flow Rate (MGD — Million Gallons per Day)
description: The volumetric flow rate through the treatment unit at the time of CT calculation, expressed in MGD (Million Gallons per Day). Flow rate determines the hydraulic contact time and is used to calculate T10 for the disinfection basin. Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian flow meters.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; AWWA; OSIsoft PI Historian

description: The actual log inactivation credit achieved based on the calculated CT value, expressed as a log10 value. Derived from EPA CT tables using disinfectant type, temperature, pH, and ct_calculated. Compared against log_inactivation_required to determine compliance status.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141 Appendix C; AWWA

description: The minimum log inactivation credit required by regulation for the target organism at this treatment facility, expressed as a log10 value (e.g., 3.0 for Giardia, 4.0 for viruses). Determined by the primacy agency based on source water quality and treatment technique requirements.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; State Drinking Water Programs and Primacy Agencies

description: The timestamp when the certified operator verified and approved this CT compliance calculation record. Provides audit trail for regulatory defensibility. Null if operator_verified = False. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; State Drinking Water Programs and Primacy Agencies; ISO 9001

description: Indicates whether a certified water treatment plant operator has reviewed and verified the accuracy of this CT compliance calculation record (True = verified; False = pending verification). Required for regulatory defensibility of MOR submissions.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; State Drinking Water Programs and Primacy Agencies

description: The measured pH (Potential of Hydrogen) of the water at the time of CT calculation. pH affects disinfection efficacy, particularly for free chlorine (hypochlorous acid vs. hypochlorite ion equilibrium), and is a key input to EPA CT tables. Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian or LIMS.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; AWWA; OSIsoft PI Historian

description: The OSIsoft PI Historian tag name for the disinfectant concentration measurement used in this CT calculation (e.g., 'WTP01.CL2.RESIDUAL.MGL'). Enables direct traceability from the compliance record back to the source PI Historian time-series data point for audit and verification.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian; U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141

description: The OSIsoft PI Historian tag name for the flow rate measurement used in this CT calculation (e.g., 'WTP01.FLOW.INFLUENT.MGD'). Enables direct traceability from the compliance record back to the source PI Historian time-series data point for audit and verification.
reference: OSIsoft PI Historian; U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141

description: The EPA-assigned Public Water System Identifier (PWSID) for the water system submitting this CT compliance record. Format: 2-letter state code followed by 7-digit system number (e.g., 'CA0100001'). Required on all regulatory submissions to EPA and state primacy agencies.
reference: U.S. EPA Safe Drinking Water Information System (SDWIS); SDWA; State Drinking Water Programs

description: Externally-known business identifier for this CT compliance calculation record, formatted as CT-{YYYY}-{MM}-{NNNNNN}. Used in Monthly Operating Reports (MOR) and EPA primacy agency submissions to uniquely reference a specific compliance calculation event.
reference: U.S. EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR) 40 CFR Part 141; State Drinking Water Programs and Primacy Agencies

description: The end date of the regulatory reporting period to which this CT compliance record belongs. Together with reporting_period_start, defines the MOR submission window for CT compliance data to the EPA primacy agency.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; State Drinking Water Programs and Primacy Agencies

description: The start date of the regulatory reporting period (typically monthly) to which this CT compliance record belongs. Used for aggregating CT compliance data into Monthly Operating Reports (MOR) and Discharge Monitoring Reports (DMR) submitted to the primacy agency.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; State Drinking Water Programs and Primacy Agencies

description: Classification of the source water type subject to SWTR CT requirements. 'surface_water' = direct surface water intake; 'groundwater_under_direct_influence' = GWUDI subject to SWTR; 'purchased_surface' = purchased water from surface water source. Determines applicable CT regulatory requirements.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; State Drinking Water Programs and Primacy Agencies

description: The method used to establish the T10 hydraulic contact time for the disinfection basin. 'tracer_study' = validated tracer study per EPA guidance; 'baffling_factor' = EPA baffling factor table applied to theoretical detention time; 'cfd_model' = computational fluid dynamics model; 'conservative_estimate' = conservative regulatory default.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR Guidance Manual; AWWA; State Drinking Water Programs

description: The pathogenic organism for which the CT log inactivation credit is being calculated. 'giardia' = Giardia lamblia (3-log inactivation required under SWTR); 'virus' = enteric viruses (4-log inactivation); 'cryptosporidium' = Cryptosporidium parvum (LT2ESWTR); 'combined' = combined credit calculation.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; U.S. EPA LT2ESWTR 40 CFR Part 141 Subpart W

description: The measured water temperature in degrees Celsius at the time of CT calculation. Temperature is a critical parameter in EPA CT tables — lower temperatures require higher CT values to achieve the same log inactivation credit. Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141 Appendix C; AWWA; OSIsoft PI Historian

description: The theoretical hydraulic detention time (volume / flow rate) of the disinfection contact basin in minutes, before applying the baffling factor to derive T10. Used in conjunction with baffling_factor to calculate contact_time_min.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR Guidance Manual; AWWA

description: The measured turbidity of the water entering the disinfection stage, expressed in NTU (Nephelometric Turbidity Units). High turbidity can shield pathogens from disinfectants, reducing CT effectiveness. Regulatory trigger: turbidity > 1 NTU may require additional CT credit. Sourced from SCADA/PI Historian or LIMS.
reference: U.S. EPA SWTR 40 CFR Part 141; AWWA; OSIsoft PI Historian

description: Audit timestamp indicating when this CT compliance record was last modified, such as after operator review, data correction, or status change. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

The measured UV (Ultraviolet Disinfection) dose delivered by the UV reactor, expressed in mJ/cm² (millijoules per square centimeter). Applicable only when disinfectant_type = 'uv'. UV dose replaces concentration-based CT for UV disinfection compliance under SWTR and LT2ESWTR. Sourced from UV reactor SCADA/PI Historian.
reference: U.S. EPA LT2ESWTR 40 CFR Part 141 Subpart W; AWWA; OSIsoft PI Historian

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the treatment operating permit record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the treatment_permit data product.
reference: Water Utilities Enterprise Data Model Convention

description: Reference to the treatment facility (Water Treatment Plant or Wastewater Treatment Plant) to which this permit is issued. Links the permit to its regulated facility.
reference: EPA NPDES Program; SDWA Operating Permit Requirements

description: Reference to the regulatory agency that issued this permit (e.g., EPA Region, State Primacy Agency, State Environmental Department). Used for compliance tracking and correspondence routing.
reference: EPA; State Drinking Water Primacy Agencies; NPDES Program

description: Indicates whether this permit includes authorization for biosolids or residuals management (land application, incineration, or landfill disposal). Relevant for WWTPs subject to EPA 40 CFR Part 503 biosolids regulations.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 503 (Biosolids Standards); CWA Section 405; WEF

description: Permitted maximum concentration of Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD) in treated effluent, expressed in milligrams per liter (mg/L). A primary indicator of organic pollution load in wastewater discharge. Used for DMR compliance evaluation.
reference: EPA Secondary Treatment Standards 40 CFR Part 133; CWA Section 301; NPDES Effluent Limitations

description: Minimum or maximum permitted chlorine residual concentration in finished water or treated effluent, expressed in mg/L. For drinking water, a minimum residual is required at the point of entry to the distribution system. For NPDES discharge, a maximum may be set to protect aquatic life.
reference: SDWA 40 CFR Part 141.72 (Disinfection); EPA NPDES Aquatic Toxicity Limits; AWWA

description: The final date by which the facility must achieve full compliance with all permit limits per the regulatory-approved compliance schedule. Null if no compliance schedule is in effect. Used to track CIP project milestones and regulatory commitments.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 122.47; State Primacy Agency Compliance Schedule Requirements

description: Indicates whether the permit includes a formal compliance schedule (a regulatory-approved timeline for achieving permit limits that the facility cannot currently meet). True triggers tracking of interim milestones and final compliance dates in the compliance management system.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 122.47 (Compliance Schedules); SDWA; State Primacy Agency Rules

description: Minimum required CT (Contact Time) value for disinfection as specified in the permit, expressed in mg·min/L (product of disinfectant concentration and contact time). CT compliance is a core Surface Water Treatment Rule requirement for Giardia and virus inactivation credit.
reference: EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR); LT2ESWTR; SDWA 40 CFR Part 141.72; CT Guidance Manual

description: The required or preferred method for submitting Discharge Monitoring Reports (DMRs) or Monthly Operating Reports (MORs) to the regulatory agency. NetDMR is the EPA's electronic DMR system; eDMR refers to state-specific electronic systems; paper and state_portal are alternatives.
reference: EPA NetDMR Program; 40 CFR Part 127 (NPDES Electronic Reporting Rule); State Primacy Agency

description: Document management system reference, file path, or URL pointing to the official permit document, fact sheet, and associated attachments stored in the enterprise document repository. Enables direct access to the authoritative permit text for compliance review.
reference: Water Utilities Document Management Policy; AWWA Compliance Records Management

description: The date on which the permit conditions become legally binding and enforceable. May differ from the issue date if a grace period or administrative continuance applies.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 122.21; State Primacy Agency Permit Conditions

description: The date on which the permit term expires. Triggers renewal workflow and compliance calendar alerts. Under administrative continuance provisions, operations may continue under the expired permit while renewal is pending.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 122.6 (Administrative Continuance); SDWA; CWA Section 402

description: Classification of the treatment facility covered by this permit. Water Treatment Plant (WTP) produces finished drinking water; Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP) or Sewage Treatment Plant (STP) treats and discharges wastewater; pump_station may hold ancillary permits; combined covers co-located facilities.
reference: EPA NPDES Program; SDWA; AWWA; WEF

description: Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) for Haloacetic Acids (HAA5) as specified in the permit or applicable regulatory standard, expressed in micrograms per liter (µg/L). The EPA MCL for HAA5 is 60 µg/L. A key Disinfection Byproduct (DBP) compliance parameter for drinking water permits.
reference: EPA Stage 2 DBPR; SDWA 40 CFR Part 141.64; HAA5 MCL

description: The date on which the regulatory agency formally issued or reissued the permit. Marks the start of the current permit term and is used to calculate permit age and renewal lead times.
reference: EPA NPDES Permit Administration; SDWA; CWA

description: Date of the most recent regulatory compliance inspection conducted by the issuing agency or primacy agency at the permitted facility. Used to track inspection frequency, prepare for upcoming inspections, and assess compliance standing.
reference: EPA NPDES Compliance Inspection Program; SDWA Sanitary Survey Requirements; State Primacy Agency

description: Required frequency of effluent or finished water quality monitoring as specified in the permit. Drives the sampling schedule in the LIMS and the reporting calendar for DMR/MOR submissions. Represents the most frequent monitoring requirement across all parameters.
reference: EPA NPDES Monitoring Requirements; SDWA 40 CFR Part 141; State Primacy Agency Monitoring Plans

description: Anticipated or scheduled date of the next regulatory compliance inspection. Supports proactive compliance preparation and internal audit scheduling. May be estimated based on inspection frequency requirements.
reference: EPA NPDES Compliance Inspection Program; SDWA Sanitary Survey Schedule; State Primacy Agency

description: Free-text field for additional operational notes, regulatory correspondence summaries, internal compliance observations, or context not captured in structured fields. Used by compliance staff to document permit history, agency communications, and action items.
reference: Water Utilities Compliance Management Policy; AWWA Compliance Records Management

business_glossary_term: National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (NPDES) Major/Minor Classification
description: EPA classification of the NPDES permit as major or minor based on facility size, discharge volume, and potential environmental impact. Major facilities are subject to more stringent oversight, mandatory DMR reporting, and EPA inspection priorities. Applicable only to NPDES permit types.
reference: EPA NPDES Program; 40 CFR Part 122; EPA ICIS-NPDES Classification Criteria

description: Indicates whether the permit is an individual permit (facility-specific terms and conditions), a general permit (covers a category of similar dischargers), or an emergency permit (temporary authorization). Determines the regulatory review process and compliance obligations.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 122.28 (General Permits); SDWA; State Primacy Agency Rules

description: Descriptive name or title of the permit as assigned by the issuing agency or used internally (e.g., 'City Water Treatment Plant NPDES Discharge Permit', 'Regional WTP Drinking Water Operating Permit'). Supports human-readable identification in compliance registers and reports.
reference: Water Utilities Internal Permit Register; AWWA Compliance Management Best Practices

description: Externally-assigned permit number as issued by the regulatory authority (e.g., NPDES permit number, state drinking water operating permit number). This is the official identifier used in all regulatory correspondence, DMRs, MORs, and CCRs.
reference: EPA NPDES Program; SDWA; CWA; State Primacy Agency Permit Numbering

description: Current lifecycle status of the permit. Active indicates the permit is in force; pending_renewal indicates the renewal application has been submitted and the permit is administratively continued; expired indicates the permit term has lapsed without renewal; suspended or revoked indicates regulatory action has been taken.
reference: EPA NPDES Permit Administration; SDWA Operating Permit Lifecycle; State Primacy Agency Rules

description: Classification of the permit by regulatory program. NPDES governs wastewater discharge under the Clean Water Act; drinking_water_operating governs WTP operations under SDWA; air_emission covers process air releases; residuals_management covers biosolids/sludge disposal; industrial_pretreatment covers IUP programs; stormwater covers MS4 or industrial stormwater. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NPDES|drinking_water_operating|air_emission|residuals_management|industrial_pretreatment|stormwater|other — promote to reference product]
reference: CWA Section 402 (NPDES); SDWA; EPA 40 CFR Parts 122-125; State Primacy Agency Regulations

business_glossary_term: Permitted Average Flow (Million Gallons per Day)
description: Monthly average flow rate authorized under this permit in Million Gallons per Day (MGD). Distinct from the peak daily limit; used for long-term capacity planning and average-basis compliance evaluation in DMR submissions.
reference: EPA NPDES Effluent Limitations Guidelines; 40 CFR Part 122 Appendix A

description: Maximum daily flow rate authorized under this permit, expressed in Million Gallons per Day (MGD). This is the principal quantitative limit governing facility capacity and is used for capacity planning, compliance monitoring, and MOR/DMR reporting.
reference: EPA NPDES Effluent Limitations; SDWA Maximum Contaminant Levels; AWWA M1 Manual

business_glossary_term: Per- and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances (PFAS) Monitoring Required Flag
description: Indicates whether the permit requires monitoring for Per- and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances (PFAS) compounds. Reflects EPA's PFAS Action Plan and emerging state-level PFAS MCLs. Drives LIMS sampling plan configuration and CCR disclosure requirements.

description: Maximum permitted pH value for treated effluent or finished water as specified in the permit. Used alongside ph_limit_min to define the acceptable pH range for compliance monitoring and corrosion control program evaluation.

description: Minimum permitted pH value for treated effluent or finished water as specified in the permit. pH is a key indicator of water chemistry and corrosion control. Typically 6.0–9.0 for NPDES effluent; 6.5–8.5 for drinking water under SDWA.
reference: EPA Secondary Treatment Standards; SDWA 40 CFR Part 141.21; NPDES Effluent Limitations

description: Name of the surface water body (river, lake, estuary, or ocean) designated as the receiving water for permitted discharges (e.g., 'Ohio River', 'Lake Michigan'). Required for NPDES permits; used in water quality impact assessments and antidegradation analysis.

description: The date on which the permit renewal application was submitted to the issuing regulatory agency. NPDES regulations typically require submission at least 180 days before expiration. Used to verify timely renewal and administrative continuance eligibility.

description: Current status of the permit renewal process. Tracks progression from initial application through agency review to final determination. Supports compliance calendar management and regulatory relationship tracking.
reference: EPA NPDES Permit Renewal Process; SDWA Operating Permit Renewal; AWWA Compliance Management

description: Required frequency for submitting compliance reports (DMRs, MORs, or equivalent) to the regulatory agency as specified in the permit. Drives the compliance reporting calendar and submission deadlines tracked in the MOR submission workflow.
reference: EPA NPDES DMR Reporting Requirements; SDWA MOR Requirements; 40 CFR Part 122.41(l)

description: Narrative description of any special conditions, schedules of compliance, best management practices (BMPs), or additional requirements imposed by the regulatory agency beyond standard effluent limitations. Examples include PFAS monitoring requirements, CSO long-term control plan milestones, or LCRR service line inventory obligations.

description: Duration of the permit in years as specified by the issuing agency. NPDES permits are typically issued for 5-year terms; state drinking water operating permits may vary. Used for renewal planning and compliance scheduling.

description: Permitted maximum concentration of Total Suspended Solids (TSS) in treated effluent, expressed in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Core secondary treatment standard for WWTP NPDES compliance. Used in DMR reporting and effluent quality monitoring.

description: Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) for Total Trihalomethanes (TTHMs) as specified in the permit or applicable regulatory standard, expressed in micrograms per liter (µg/L). reference: EPA Stage 2 Disinfectants and Disinfection Byproducts Rule (Stage 2 DBPR); SDWA 40 CFR Part 141.64; MCL

description: Maximum permitted turbidity of finished water or treated effluent in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU). For WTPs, the Surface Water Treatment Rule requires combined filter effluent turbidity not to exceed 0.3 NTU in 95% of measurements. Critical for MCL compliance.
reference: EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR); LT2ESWTR; 40 CFR Part 141.73; SDWA MCL

description: Number of currently unresolved permit violations (effluent limit exceedances, reporting violations, or schedule of compliance violations) associated with this permit. Sourced from the regulatory agency's compliance tracking system. Used for compliance risk scoring and executive reporting.
reference: EPA ECHO (Enforcement and Compliance History Online); NPDES Compliance Tracking; State Agency

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each treatment regulatory violation record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.

business_glossary_term: National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (NPDES) Permit ID
description: Reference to the NPDES permit under which the violation was recorded, applicable for wastewater discharge violations at WWTP facilities.
reference: EPA CWA Section 402; NPDES Permit Program

description: Reference to the IBM Maximo CMMS work order created to execute the corrective action plan for this violation. Links compliance management to maintenance and operations workflows.

description: EPA or state-assigned contaminant code for the parameter that was violated (e.g., TTHM for Total Trihalomethanes, PFAS, turbidity, lead, copper, BOD, TSS). Aligns with EPA SDWIS contaminant codes.

description: Full descriptive name of the contaminant or treatment parameter involved in the violation (e.g., Total Trihalomethanes, Turbidity, Lead, Per- and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances (PFAS), Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD)).

description: Narrative description of the corrective action plan developed to address the root cause of the violation and restore compliance. Includes immediate remediation steps, process adjustments, and preventive measures.

description: The date and time when this violation record was first created in the compliance management system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: Water Utilities Internal Data Standards; ISO 9001 Quality Management; GASB

description: Precise date and time when the violation was detected, either through SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian alarm, laboratory result, or operator observation. Supports CT compliance and public notification deadline calculations.

description: The DMR reporting period (YYYY-MM) in which this violation must be included for NPDES permit compliance reporting. Applicable for WWTP effluent violations. Links to the corresponding DMR submission.

description: Type of formal enforcement action taken by the regulatory agency (e.g., Notice of Violation (NOV), Administrative Order (AO), Consent Order, Civil Penalty, Criminal Referral). reference: EPA SDWA; EPA Enforcement and Compliance Assurance; State Primacy Agency Programs

description: The numeric difference between the measured value and the regulatory limit (measured_value minus regulatory_limit). Positive values indicate the degree of exceedance above the limit, supporting severity assessment and trend analysis.
Type of treatment facility where the violation occurred: WTP (Water Treatment Plant), WWTP (Wastewater Treatment Plant), STP (Sewage Treatment Plant), booster station, or storage facility.

Indicates whether this violation is a repeat occurrence of the same contaminant or parameter violation at the same facility within the preceding 12-month period. Repeat violations may trigger escalated regulatory response and increased penalties.
reference: EPA SDWA; State Primacy Agency Programs; AWWA Compliance Analytics

description: The actual measured concentration, level, or parameter value recorded at the time of the violation (e.g., turbidity in NTU, chlorine residual in mg/L, BOD in mg/L, CT value in mg·min/L). This is the quantitative result that triggered the violation.
reference: EPA SDWA 40 CFR Part 141; EPA NPDES; LIMS (LabWare)

description: Unit of measure for the measured_value field (e.g., mg/L for chemical concentrations, NTU for Nephelometric Turbidity Units, mg·min/L for CT Contact Time, CFU/100mL for microbial counts). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mg/L|NTU|mg·min/L|CFU/100mL|pH|mg/kg|ug/L|pCi/L|MPN/100mL — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: EPA SDWA 40 CFR Part 141; AWWA; ISO 9001

description: The MOR reporting period (YYYY-MM) in which this violation must be included for regulatory submission. Links the violation to the corresponding Monthly Operating Report for the facility.
reference: EPA SDWA; State Primacy Agency MOR Requirements; AWWA

description: Free-text field for the certified treatment plant operator to record observations, circumstances, and contextual information about the violation at the time of detection or during investigation.

description: Monetary penalty assessed by the regulatory agency for this violation, in USD. Supports financial liability tracking, OPEX reporting, and GAAP/GASB financial disclosures for municipal utilities.
reference: EPA SDWA; GAAP; GASB; State Primacy Agency Programs

description: The operating permit number (e.g., NPDES permit number, state drinking water permit) under which the facility operates and against which the violation is recorded. Used in regulatory correspondence and DMR/MOR submissions.
reference: EPA NPDES; EPA SDWA; State Primacy Agency Programs

description: The date on which public notification was issued to customers and the public in accordance with the EPA Public Notification Rule. Null if notification has not yet been issued.
reference: EPA SDWA 40 CFR Part 141 Subpart Q; Oracle Utilities CC&B

description: Indicates whether this violation triggers a mandatory public notification requirement under EPA SDWA Public Notification Rule (40 CFR Part 141 Subpart Q). True when the violation type and severity mandate customer and public notification.
reference: EPA SDWA 40 CFR Part 141 Subpart Q; Public Notification Rule

description: EPA Public Notification Rule tier classification: Tier 1 requires notification within 24 hours (acute health risk); Tier 2 requires notification within 30 days; Tier 3 requires annual notification. Drives notification workflow and deadline management.
reference: EPA SDWA 40 CFR Part 141 Subpart Q; Public Notification Rule

description: The applicable Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL), treatment technique standard, or permit limit that was exceeded or violated. For CT violations, this is the required CT value in mg·min/L.
reference: EPA SDWA 40 CFR Part 141; EPA NPDES; AWWA; LCRR

description: Classification of the regulatory standard that was violated: MCL (Maximum Contaminant Level), MCLG (Maximum Contaminant Level Goal), treatment technique standard, NPDES permit limit, action level (e.g., Lead and Copper Rule), or required CT (Contact Time for Disinfection).
reference: EPA SDWA 40 CFR Part 141; EPA NPDES; LCRR; AWWA

description: The date on which the regulatory agency (EPA or State Primacy Agency) was formally notified of this violation, as required by SDWA or NPDES permit conditions.
reference: EPA SDWA; EPA NPDES; State Primacy Agency Programs

description: Status of the formal notification to the regulatory agency (EPA or State Primacy Agency) regarding this violation. Tracks whether notification is pending, has been submitted, has been acknowledged, or is overdue per regulatory deadlines.
reference: EPA SDWA; State Primacy Agency Programs; NACWA

description: The date on which the violation was resolved and compliance was restored, confirmed by a return-to-compliance sample result or regulatory acknowledgment. Null if the violation remains open.
reference: EPA SDWA; AWWA; State Primacy Agency Programs

description: Categorical classification of the root cause of the violation to support trend analysis and preventive action programs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equipment_failure|process_upset|operator_error|source_water_event|chemical_dosing|monitoring_failure|reporting_failure|infrastructure|other — promote to reference product]
reference: AWWA; WEF; ISO 14001 Environmental Management; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Assessed severity classification of the violation based on the degree of exceedance, public health risk, and regulatory consequence. Critical indicates immediate public health risk; high indicates significant exceedance; medium indicates moderate concern; low indicates minor or administrative violation.
reference: EPA SDWA; AWWA Compliance Management Best Practices; State Primacy Agency Programs

description: The date and time when this violation record was last modified in the compliance management system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and incremental Silver Layer processing.
reference: Water Utilities Internal Data Standards; ISO 9001 Quality Management; GASB

description: The calendar date on which the treatment violation or regulatory exceedance was first identified or occurred, as recorded in the compliance management system.
Externally-known, human-readable unique reference number assigned to this violation record, used in regulatory correspondence, public notifications, and compliance tracking systems.
reference: EPA Safe Drinking Water Information System (SDWIS); AWWA

description: Current lifecycle status of the violation record, tracking its progression from identification through corrective action to regulatory closure.
reference: EPA SDWIS; AWWA Compliance Management Best Practices

description: Classification of the regulatory violation category. MCL exceedance indicates a Maximum Contaminant Level was exceeded; treatment technique indicates a required process was not followed; monitoring and reporting indicates a missed sample or late report; CT violation indicates Contact Time for disinfection was insufficient; turbidity violation indicates NTU limits were breached. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mcl_exceedance|treatment_technique|monitoring_reporting|ct_violation|turbidity_violation|public_notification|other — promote to reference product]

description: Reference to the specific I&I monitoring point where this flow measurement was captured.

Reference to the specific sewer network segment (pipe, manhole, or structure) associated with this monitoring point.
Indicates whether this measurement triggered an alarm condition in the SCADA system due to exceeding thresholds or anomalous patterns.
Type of alarm triggered by this measurement: high flow, low flow, sensor fault, communication loss, or none.

business_glossary_term: Average Flow Rate in Gallons Per Minute (GPM)
description: Average flow rate over the measurement period, expressed in gallons per minute.
reference: AWWA M36 Water Audits and Loss Control Programs

business_glossary_term: Calculated Inflow and Infiltration (I&I) Volume in Gallons
description: Calculated volume of inflow and infiltration for the measurement period, derived by subtracting the dry weather baseline from the measured flow and integrating over time.

description: Date when the flow sensor was last calibrated prior to this measurement, ensuring measurement accuracy.

description: Quality indicator for the measurement data: valid (passed all checks), suspect (questionable but usable), invalid (failed validation), estimated (imputed value), or missing (no data captured).

description: Free-text notes explaining data quality issues, sensor malfunctions, calibration events, or other factors affecting measurement reliability.
reference: EPA Quality Assurance Project Plan (QAPP) Guidelines

description: Origin of the measurement data: SCADA system, manual field reading, mobile device, or third-party monitoring service.

business_glossary_term: Dry Weather Baseline Flow Rate in Gallons Per Minute (GPM)
description: Established baseline flow rate during dry weather conditions at this monitoring point, used as the reference for calculating I&I contributions.

description: Classification of the I&I contribution type: infiltration (groundwater seepage), inflow (direct stormwater entry), combined (both), or unknown.

business_glossary_term: Measured Flow Rate in Gallons Per Minute (GPM)

business_glossary_term: Measured Flow Rate in Million Gallons per Day (MGD)
description: Instantaneous flow rate measured at the monitoring point expressed in million gallons per day.

description: Duration over which the flow measurement was averaged or integrated, expressed in minutes.
Technology or method used to capture the flow measurement: ultrasonic, magnetic flow meter, weir, flume, or manual reading.

description: Date and time when the flow measurement was recorded at the monitoring point.

business_glossary_term: Minimum Flow Rate in Gallons Per Minute (GPM)

business_glossary_term: Peak Flow Rate in Gallons Per Minute (GPM)

description: Material composition of the force main pipe: ductile iron (DI), polyvinyl chloride (PVC), high-density polyethylene (HDPE), steel, or concrete. Impacts corrosion resistance, lifespan, and maintenance requirements.

description: Date when the lift station was originally installed and commissioned for service. Used for asset age calculation, depreciation, and replacement planning.

description: Timestamp when this lift station record was last updated. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and change tracking.
Date of the most recent major rehabilitation or upgrade of the lift station. Used to track asset condition and plan future capital improvements.
reference: ISO 55001 Asset Management; WEF Manual of Practice No. 9

description: Geographic latitude coordinate of the lift station in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for GIS mapping, asset location, and emergency response.
Geographic longitude coordinate of the lift station in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for GIS mapping, asset location, and emergency response.
Date of the next planned preventive maintenance activity for the lift station. Used for work order scheduling and resource planning.
reference: IBM Maximo CMMS; WEF Manual of Practice No. 9

description: Free-text field for additional operational notes, special conditions, historical context, or site-specific information about the lift station.
reference: IBM Maximo CMMS; WEF Manual of Practice No. 9

description: Total count of pumps installed at the lift station, including duty pumps and standby/backup units. Critical for redundancy planning and maintenance scheduling.
reference: WEF Manual of Practice No. 9; IBM Maximo CMMS

description: Current operational state of the lift station in its lifecycle: active (in service), inactive (temporarily offline), standby (backup), maintenance (under repair), decommissioned (retired), or under construction (not yet commissioned).

description: Legal ownership classification of the lift station: utility owned (owned and operated by the water utility), private (privately owned), developer (owned by developer, pending transfer), municipal (owned by municipality), or joint (shared ownership). Impacts maintenance responsibility and capital planning.

Pump redundancy configuration: simplex (1 pump, no backup), duplex (2 pumps, 1 duty + 1 standby), triplex (3 pumps), quadruplex (4 pumps). Determines operational reliability and maintenance flexibility.
reference: WEF Manual of Practice No. 9; AWWA M51

description: Total rated horsepower (HP) of the primary duty pump(s). Used for energy consumption analysis and operational cost estimation.
reference: WEF Manual of Practice No. 9; AWWA M51

description: Name or designation of the geographic service area or collection basin served by this lift station. Used for operational planning and customer communication.

description: Estimated population served by the lift station within its collection basin. Used for capacity planning, regulatory reporting, and capital improvement prioritization.

description: State or province where the lift station is located. Used for regulatory reporting to state primacy agencies and EPA.

description: Unique alphanumeric code or tag assigned to the lift station for asset tracking, SCADA integration, and maintenance management (CMMS).

description: Business name or designation of the lift station for operational reference and public communication.
reference: AWWA Asset Management Standards; WEF Manual of Practice No. 9

description: Classification of the lift station based on pump configuration and design: submersible (pumps submerged in wet well), dry pit (pumps in separate dry chamber), wet pit (pumps in wet well but accessible), pneumatic (air-pressure driven), or grinder (with grinder pumps for solids reduction).

business_glossary_term: Manhole Assessment and Certification Program (MACP) Score
description: Numerical condition score assigned using the NASSCO Manhole Assessment and Certification Program (MACP) methodology. Higher scores indicate worse condition. Used for prioritizing rehabilitation and capital planning.
reference: NASSCO MACP (Manhole Assessment and Certification Program)

description: Business identifier or asset tag assigned to the manhole for field operations and maintenance tracking. Typically displayed on manhole covers or in field maps.

reference: NASSCO MACP (Manhole Assessment and Certification Program)

description: Classification of the manhole based on its function in the wastewater collection network. Entity that owns the manhole asset. Utility-owned assets are maintained by the water utility; municipal assets may be owned by the city; private assets are on private property; joint ownership involves shared responsibility.
Postal code of the manhole location. Used for geographic segmentation and service area mapping.

Street address or nearest intersection where the manhole is located. Used for work order dispatch and public communication.

Load rating classification of the manhole cover based on expected vehicular traffic. Light duty for pedestrian areas; medium duty for residential streets; heavy duty for arterial roads; extra heavy duty for highways and industrial areas.

description: Timestamp when this manhole record was last updated in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail.

Foreign key reference to the maintenance work order or service request that triggered this inspection, linking inspection to asset management workflows.

Weather conditions at the time of inspection (dry, wet, recent rain), relevant for interpreting infiltration and flow observations.

description: Pricing model applied to the offering. Unique identifier for the debt instrument record in the water utility's long-term debt portfolio.

The structure of principal repayment over the life of the debt. Level principal has equal principal payments; level debt service has equal combined principal and interest payments; bullet has single principal payment at maturity; serial has staggered maturities; term has single maturity date.
reference: GASB Statement 62 - Codification of Accounting and Financial Reporting Guidance

description: The name of the law firm that provided the legal opinion on the validity and tax-exempt status of the debt issuance.

description: The credit quality rating assigned to the debt instrument by rating agencies (e.g., Moody's, S&P, Fitch). Common ratings include AAA, AA, A, BBB, etc. Higher ratings indicate lower credit risk and typically result in lower interest costs.
reference: Municipal Securities Rulemaking Board (MSRB) Rule G-17

business_glossary_term: Comprehensive Annual Financial Report (CAFR) Reporting Category
description: The category under which this debt instrument is reported in the utility's CAFR. Water utilities typically report under business-type activities as enterprise funds.
The earliest date on which the issuer may exercise the call option to redeem the debt instrument before maturity. Null if the instrument is non-callable.
reference: GASB Statement 62 - Codification of Accounting and Financial Reporting Guidance

description: The premium above par value that the issuer must pay to bondholders if the debt is called before maturity, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 2.0000 represents 2.00% premium). Null if non-callable.
reference: GASB Statement 62 - Codification of Accounting and Financial Reporting Guidance

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the issuer has the right to redeem (call) the debt instrument before its stated maturity date. True indicates callable; False indicates non-callable.
reference: GASB Statement 62 - Codification of Accounting and Financial Reporting Guidance

description: The frequency at which interest payments (coupons) are made to bondholders or lenders. Semi-annual is most common for municipal bonds.
reference: GASB Statement 62 - Codification of Accounting and Financial Reporting Guidance

description: Description of financial covenants and restrictions imposed by the debt agreement, such as debt service coverage ratio requirements, rate covenant obligations, reserve fund requirements, or restrictions on additional debt issuance.
reference: GASB Statement 62 - Codification of Accounting and Financial Reporting Guidance

description: The timestamp when this debt instrument record was first created in the system.
reference: GASB Statement 62 - Codification of Accounting and Financial Reporting Guidance

business_glossary_term: Committee on Uniform Securities Identification Procedures (CUSIP) Number
description: The 9-character alphanumeric identifier assigned to the debt instrument for trading and settlement purposes in the securities market. Format: 3 digits (issuer), 6 alphanumeric (issue), 1 check digit.
reference: Municipal Securities Rulemaking Board (MSRB) Rule G-34

description: The required balance to be maintained in the debt service reserve fund as specified in the bond indenture, typically equal to maximum annual debt service or a percentage of outstanding principal.
reference: GASB Statement 62 - Codification of Accounting and Financial Reporting Guidance

description: The classification of the debt instrument for GASB financial reporting purposes. Long-term debt has maturity beyond one year; short-term debt matures within one year; current portion represents principal due within the next fiscal year; non-current portion represents principal due beyond the next fiscal year.
The externally-known unique identifier or serial number assigned to the debt instrument by the issuer, trustee, or financial institution (e.g., bond CUSIP, loan agreement number).
reference: GASB Statement 62 - Codification of Accounting and Financial Reporting Guidance

description: Current lifecycle status of the debt instrument. Active indicates the debt is outstanding and serviced; matured indicates principal fully repaid at term end; defeased indicates legally satisfied through irrevocable trust; refunded indicates replaced by new debt issuance; defaulted indicates payment obligations not met; cancelled indicates instrument voided before issuance.
reference: GASB Statement 7 - Advance Refundings Resulting in Defeasance of Debt

description: Classification of the debt instrument by its legal and financial structure. Revenue bonds are secured by utility revenues; general obligation bonds by taxing authority; SRF (State Revolving Fund) loans provide low-cost financing for water infrastructure; WIFIA (Water Infrastructure Finance and Innovation Act) loans offer federal credit assistance; commercial paper provides short-term financing; bank loans are direct borrowings from financial institutions.
reference: GASB Statement 34 - Basic Financial Statements and Management's Discussion and Analysis for State and Local Governments

description: The name of the bond insurance company providing credit enhancement for the debt instrument, if applicable. Bond insurance guarantees timely payment of principal and interest in case of issuer default.
reference: Municipal Securities Rulemaking Board (MSRB) Rule G-17

description: The day-count convention used to calculate accrued interest. 30/360 assumes 30-day months and 360-day years; Actual/360 uses actual days and 360-day years; Actual/365 uses actual days and 365-day years; Actual/Actual uses actual days and actual year length.
reference: Municipal Securities Rulemaking Board (MSRB) Rule G-33

description: The annual interest rate applied to the outstanding principal balance, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 3.5000 represents 3.50%). For variable rate instruments, this represents the current rate.
reference: GASB Statement 62 - Codification of Accounting and Financial Reporting Guidance

description: Classification of the interest rate structure. Fixed rates remain constant over the life of the instrument; variable rates adjust periodically based on market indices; zero coupon instruments accrue interest but pay at maturity.
reference: GASB Statement 53 - Accounting and Financial Reporting for Derivative Instruments

description: The 12-character alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the debt instrument in international securities markets. Format: 2-letter country code + 9-character national identifier + 1 check digit.
The date on which the debt instrument was originally issued and funds were received by the water utility.
reference: GASB Statement 62 - Codification of Accounting and Financial Reporting Guidance

description: For variable rate demand obligations, the name of the financial institution providing the standby bond purchase agreement or letter of credit to ensure liquidity if bonds cannot be remarketed.
reference: GASB Statement 62 - Codification of Accounting and Financial Reporting Guidance

description: The date on which the final principal payment is due and the debt instrument obligation is fully satisfied.
The timestamp when this debt instrument record was last modified or updated in the system.
reference: GASB Statement 62 - Codification of Accounting and Financial Reporting Guidance

description: The total face value or principal amount of the debt instrument at issuance, before any payments or amortization.
The current unpaid principal balance remaining on the debt instrument after all principal payments to date.
The name of the financial institution designated to distribute principal and interest payments to bondholders on behalf of the issuer.
reference: Municipal Securities Rulemaking Board (MSRB) Rule G-34

description: Detailed description of the specific capital projects or initiatives funded by this debt instrument, including water treatment plant upgrades, distribution network expansion, wastewater treatment improvements, or other infrastructure investments.
The intended use of proceeds from the debt issuance, such as capital improvement projects (CIP), infrastructure replacement, system expansion, refunding existing debt, or working capital needs.
reference: GASB Statement 34 - Management's Discussion and Analysis

description: Boolean flag indicating whether debt service costs for this instrument are included in the utility's rate base for rate-setting purposes. True indicates included in rates; False indicates excluded.
For variable rate debt, the frequency at which the interest rate is recalculated and adjusted based on the underlying index.
reference: GASB Statement 53 - Accounting and Financial Reporting for Derivative Instruments

description: The name of the credit rating agency that assigned the bond rating (e.g., Moody's Investors Service, Standard & Poor's, Fitch Ratings).
reference: Municipal Securities Rulemaking Board (MSRB) Rule G-17

description: For variable rate demand obligations, the name of the financial institution responsible for resetting the interest rate and remarketing bonds tendered by investors.
reference: Municipal Securities Rulemaking Board (MSRB) Rule G-17

description: The type of security or collateral backing the debt instrument. Revenue pledge is secured by utility operating revenues; general obligation by taxing power; special assessment by property assessments; moral obligation by non-binding commitment; unsecured has no specific collateral.
reference: GASB Statement 34 - Notes to Financial Statements

description: Boolean flag indicating whether interest income from this debt instrument is exempt from federal income tax. True indicates tax-exempt; False indicates taxable. Most municipal water utility bonds are tax-exempt.
reference: Internal Revenue Code Section 103 - Interest on State and Local Bonds

description: The name of the financial institution serving as trustee, responsible for safeguarding bondholders' interests, holding collateral, and ensuring compliance with bond covenants.
reference: GASB Statement 62 - Codification of Accounting and Financial Reporting Guidance

description: The name of the investment banking firm or financial institution that underwrote the debt issuance and facilitated its sale to investors.
reference: Municipal Securities Rulemaking Board (MSRB) Rule G-17

description: For variable rate debt, the market index to which the interest rate is tied, such as SOFR (Secured Overnight Financing Rate), LIBOR (being phased out), SIFMA (Securities Industry and Financial Markets Association Municipal Swap Index), or Prime Rate.
reference: GASB Statement 53 - Accounting and Financial Reporting for Derivative Instruments

description: Unique identifier for the debt service payment transaction.

description: Timestamp when the debt service payment record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the debt service payment. Typically USD for U.S. water utilities.
Number of days the debt service payment was made after the due date, if applicable.
reference: GASB Statement 62 - Codification of Accounting and Financial Reporting Guidance

description: The debt service coverage ratio calculated at the time of this payment, representing the ratio of net revenues available for debt service to the debt service payment amount, used for bond covenant monitoring.

description: The scheduled date on which the debt service payment is contractually due per the bond indenture or loan agreement.
reference: GASB Statement 62 - Codification of Accounting and Financial Reporting Guidance

description: Additional fees associated with the debt service payment, including trustee fees, paying agent fees, or administrative charges.
reference: GASB Statement 62 - Codification of Accounting and Financial Reporting Guidance

description: The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year in which the debt service payment is recorded.
The fiscal year in which the debt service payment is recorded for financial reporting and budgeting purposes.
The date on which the debt service payment transaction was posted to the general ledger in the ERP system.
The portion of the debt service payment applied to interest expense on the debt instrument.
reference: GASB Statement 62 - Codification of Accounting and Financial Reporting Guidance

description: Timestamp when the debt service payment record was last modified or updated in the system.
Boolean flag indicating whether the debt service payment was made after the contractual due date.
reference: GASB Statement 62 - Codification of Accounting and Financial Reporting Guidance

description: Penalty amount assessed for late payment of debt service, if applicable per the bond indenture or loan agreement.
reference: GASB Statement 62 - Codification of Accounting and Financial Reporting Guidance

description: Free-text notes or comments regarding the debt service payment, including any special circumstances, adjustments, or explanations.
reference: GASB Statement 62 - Codification of Accounting and Financial Reporting Guidance

description: The actual date on which the debt service payment was made or processed by the utility.
The method by which the debt service payment was transmitted to the trustee or paying agent.

description: Business identifier for the debt service payment, typically sequential or following a numbering convention established by the utility or trustee.
Current lifecycle status of the debt service payment indicating whether it is scheduled, processed, confirmed by trustee, failed, or reversed.

description: Classification of the debt service payment indicating whether it is a scheduled payment, prepayment, defeasance, refunding, or penalty payment.
reference: GASB Statement 23 - Accounting and Financial Reporting for Refundings of Debt

description: The amortization amount for bond premium or discount allocated to this payment period, following the effective interest method or straight-line method as applicable.

description: The portion of the debt service payment applied to the principal balance of the debt instrument.

Bank or financial system transaction reference number for the debt service payment, used for reconciliation and audit trail.

description: Confirmation or reference number provided by the bond trustee or paying agent acknowledging receipt and processing of the debt service payment.

description: Name of the bond trustee or paying agent responsible for receiving and disbursing the debt service payment to bondholders.
reference: GASB Statement 62 - Codification of Accounting and Financial Reporting Guidance

description: Tyler Technologies Munis system transaction identifier for the debt service payment, used for municipal utilities.
Unique identifier for the rate case proceeding. Primary key for the rate case master record.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Rate cases are managed by specific regulatory affairs or finance cost centers. Adding cost_center_id enables cost center-level rate case cost tracking and SAP CO integration. No bidirectional conflict

Foreign key linking to finance.fund. Business justification: Rate cases are filed for specific utility funds (water enterprise fund, wastewater fund). Adding fund_id links the rate case to the applicable municipal fund, enabling fund-level rate case tracking an

Foreign key linking to service.territory. Business justification: Rate cases are filed for specific service territories with the applicable regulatory authority. Multi-territory utilities manage separate rate cases per territory. Regulatory docket tracking, public n

The overall rate of return on rate base authorized by the regulatory commission, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0725 for 7.25%). Represents the weighted average cost of capital (WACC) including debt and equity components.
reference: Public Utilities Commission Rate of Return Standards

description: The authorized return on equity component of the rate of return, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0950 for 9.50%). Represents the profit margin allowed on shareholder equity investment in the utility.
reference: Public Utilities Commission Rate of Return Standards

description: Indicates whether any party filed an appeal or request for rehearing of the commission decision. Appeals may delay rate implementation or require refund provisions.

Total annual revenue requirement approved by the regulatory commission or utility board. This is the final authorized revenue level after regulatory review, hearings, and adjustments. May differ from the requested amount due to disallowed costs, rate base adjustments, or modified return on equity. Expressed in USD.
reference: Public Utilities Commission Rate Case Decision Standards

description: Total value of the Capital Improvement Program (CIP) or Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) plan supporting the rate case, covering planned infrastructure investments during the test year and rate period. Expressed in USD.
reference: American Water Works Association (AWWA) Asset Management Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the rate case proceeding. Tracks progression from initial draft through regulatory review, hearings, commission decision, and final disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|filed|pending_review|hearing_scheduled|decision_pending|approved|denied|withdrawn — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Classification of the rate case proceeding. General rate case (GRC) is a comprehensive review of revenue requirements; interim adjustments address specific cost changes; surcharges recover specific program costs; cost of service reviews cost allocation; rate design modifies rate structure without changing revenue; emergency rates address urgent financial needs.
reference: Public Utilities Commission Rate Case Classification Standards

description: Percentage change in typical commercial customer bills resulting from the approved rate case.
reference: Public Utilities Commission Rate Impact Reporting Standards

description: Name of the external consulting firm or advisor retained to prepare the rate case filing, cost of service study, and regulatory testimony. Common firms include Black & Veatch, Raftelis, NewGen Strategies, and specialized rate consultants.
reference: Internal Procurement and Vendor Management Standards

description: Timestamp when this rate case record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

The cost allocation methodology used to distribute revenue requirements across customer classes (residential, commercial, industrial, public authority). Base-extra-capacity allocates fixed costs by capacity and variable by usage; commodity-demand splits by consumption and peak demand; customer count uses simple per-account allocation; meter equivalent weights by meter size; peak demand allocates by maximum usage contribution.
reference: American Water Works Association (AWWA) M1 Manual - Principles of Water Rates, Fees, and Charges

description: Annual debt service costs (principal and interest payments) on utility bonds and loans included in the revenue requirement. Expressed in USD.
reference: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP), Governmental Accounting Standards Board (GASB)

description: Date the regulatory commission issued its final decision and order on the rate case. This date establishes the official record and triggers appeal deadlines.
Web link to the official commission decision document, order, or ruling. Typically hosted on the Public Utilities Commission website or document management system.
reference: Public Utilities Commission Electronic Filing Standards

description: Annual depreciation expense included in the revenue requirement, calculated based on approved depreciation rates and asset lives for utility plant and equipment. Expressed in USD.
reference: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP), Governmental Accounting Standards Board (GASB)

description: Official docket or case number assigned by the Public Utilities Commission (PUC) or regulatory body for tracking the rate proceeding. This is the externally-known identifier used in all regulatory filings and correspondence.
Date the rate case application was formally filed with the Public Utilities Commission or regulatory authority. This date triggers statutory review timelines and procedural deadlines.

business_glossary_term: Governmental Accounting Standards Board (GASB) Compliance Notes
description: Free-text notes documenting compliance with Governmental Accounting Standards Board (GASB) requirements for municipal utilities, including fund accounting, regulatory asset/liability treatment, and financial statement presentation.
Date of the formal public hearing before the regulatory commission where testimony is presented, witnesses are cross-examined, and public comment is received.
Percentage change in typical industrial customer bills resulting from the approved rate case.
reference: Public Utilities Commission Rate Impact Reporting Standards

description: Number of parties granted intervenor status in the rate case proceeding. Intervenors may include consumer advocates, industrial customer groups, environmental organizations, or other stakeholders with standing to participate.
Timestamp when this rate case record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Name of the lead legal counsel representing the utility in the rate case proceeding. May be in-house counsel or external regulatory law firm.

Anticipated filing date for the next general rate case. Used for financial planning and analysis (FP&A) and long-term capital planning. May be driven by regulatory requirements, financial need, or strategic timing.
Free-text field for additional context, special circumstances, regulatory conditions, or internal commentary related to the rate case proceeding.

Total annual operating expenses included in the approved revenue requirement. Includes operations and maintenance (O&M) costs, administrative expenses, purchased water, power, chemicals, labor, and other recurring costs. Expressed in USD.
reference: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP), Governmental Accounting Standards Board (GASB)

description: Date public notice of the rate case filing was published in newspapers, utility bills, or other media as required by regulatory rules. Triggers public comment period.
reference: Public Utilities Commission Public Notice Requirements

description: The net value of utility plant and equipment upon which the utility is allowed to earn a return. Calculated as gross plant in service plus working capital minus accumulated depreciation and contributions in aid of construction. Expressed in USD.
reference: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP), Governmental Accounting Standards Board (GASB)

description: The pricing methodology used to allocate the approved revenue requirement across customer classes and rate components. Cost of service allocates based on cost causation; value of service considers customer willingness to pay; marginal cost reflects incremental supply costs; tiered conservation encourages efficiency; uniform rate applies flat pricing; lifeline protects low-income customers.
reference: American Water Works Association (AWWA) M1 Manual - Principles of Water Rates, Fees, and Charges

description: Date on which the approved rates become effective and are applied to customer bills. May be subject to refund provisions during appeal periods or interim rate periods.
reference: Public Utilities Commission Rate Implementation Standards

description: Number of days between rate case filing and rate effective date. Regulatory lag represents the period during which the utility operates under outdated rates while awaiting approval, potentially impacting financial performance.

Total annual revenue requirement requested by the utility in the rate case filing. Represents the utility's calculation of the revenue needed to cover operating expenses (OPEX), capital costs (CAPEX), debt service, and allowed return on investment (ROI). Expressed in USD.
reference: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP), Governmental Accounting Standards Board (GASB)

description: Percentage change in typical residential customer bills resulting from the approved rate case. Used for public communication and affordability analysis.
reference: Public Utilities Commission Rate Impact Reporting Standards

description: Dollar amount of the approved revenue increase (or decrease if negative) compared to current authorized revenue levels. Calculated as approved revenue requirement minus current revenue requirement. Expressed in USD.
reference: Public Utilities Commission Rate Case Decision Standards

description: Percentage increase (or decrease if negative) in authorized revenue compared to current levels. Calculated as (revenue increase amount / current revenue requirement) * 100. Used for public communication and rate impact analysis.
reference: Public Utilities Commission Rate Case Decision Standards

description: Indicates whether the rate case was resolved through a negotiated settlement agreement among parties rather than a fully litigated commission decision. Settlements often result in faster resolution and reduced regulatory costs.
Ending date of the test year period used to establish the utility's revenue requirements and cost structure.
reference: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP), Governmental Accounting Standards Board (GASB)

description: Beginning date of the test year period used to establish the utility's revenue requirements and cost structure. The test year is the 12-month period (historical or forecasted) upon which rate calculations are based.
reference: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP), Governmental Accounting Standards Board (GASB)

description: Indicates whether the test year is based on historical actual data, forecasted future data, or a hybrid approach combining historical base with known adjustments. Regulatory bodies may have preferences or requirements for test year methodology.
reference: Public Utilities Commission Rate Case Standards

description: Unique identifier for the revenue requirement calculation record.

The rate of return on rate base authorized by the Public Utilities Commission (PUC), typically based on the utility's weighted average cost of capital (WACC).

description: The method used to allocate costs among customer classes, such as base-extra capacity, commodity-demand, customer count, meter equivalent, or consumption ratio.
